1	# Edge Qualifier Vocabulary
2	
3	> The Weirwood Network edge schema includes an optional `qualifier` field on emit_edge rows. Qualifier vocabulary is three-tiered:
4	> - **Tier 1 (REQUIRED enum)** — edge MUST emit a qualifier from the listed enum. Validator rejects empty or out-of-enum.
5	> - **Tier 2 (OPTIONAL enum)** — qualifier may be omitted; if emitted, must match the listed enum. Validator rejects out-of-enum but accepts omission.
6	> - **Tier 3 (no qualifier)** — DEFAULT for any edge type not listed in the table below. Edge MUST NOT have a `qualifier` field. Validator rejects any non-empty qualifier on Tier-3 edges.
7	>
8	> The `notes` field is **deleted from the schema entirely** as of 2026-05-18 (Session 57 lock). Validator rejects any edge carrying a `notes` field.
9	
10	---
11	
12	## Tier 1 — REQUIRED qualifier (8 edge types)
13	
14	| Edge Type | Enum Values | Rationale | Data Source |
15	|-----------|-------------|-----------|-------------|
16	| `SIBLING_OF` | `full`, `half`, `step`, `milk`, `unknown` | Universal Westerosi kinship category. Corpus: half-brother ×44, half-sister ×9, milk-brother ×9, step- ×0. Step is included as a defensive option (Tommen/Robert via Cersei's prior marriage etc., though rare). | Pass 1 corpus + ASOIAF series knowledge |
17	| `SPOUSE_OF` | `current`, `former`, `annulled`, `widowed`, `salt_wife`, `unknown` | Four+1 canonical marital states. Corpus: widow* ×238, first/second/third wife ×92, former wife ×9. Wiki: salt wife ×5, dissolved ×3, annulled ×1, possibly ×6. `salt_wife` captures the Ironborn quaternary marital institution. | Wiki infobox + corpus |
18	| `PARENT_OF` | `biological`, `adopted`, `claimed`, `rumored`, `disputed`, `unknown` | Captures legitimacy / certainty states. Wiki: rumored ×34, disputed ×20, officially ×16, possibly ×13, adopted ×4. `biological` is the default; `claimed` covers Aegon-VI-Targaryen-style assertions; `rumored` covers folkloric attestation. WARD_OF/FOSTERED_BY are separate (fostering is not parentage). | Wiki infobox `Fathers`/`Mothers` plural fields + corpus |
19	| `WARD_OF` | `formal`, `informal`, `hostage`, `unknown` | Three distinct fostership states in Westerosi politics. Corpus: ward ×259, fostered ×47, hostage ×381. Hostage-as-ward is the dark form (Theon under Ned); formal = acknowledged fostership (Robert under Jon Arryn); informal = household upbringing without formal compact. | Pass 1 corpus + series knowledge |
20	| `HOLDS_TITLE` | `current`, `former`, `claimed`, `contested`, `historical`, `unknown` | Highest-volume qualifier-bearing field in wiki: 256 qualifier rows / 3976 edges. Wiki: formerly ×62, claimant ×57, historical ×35, stripped ×7, self-styled ×4, disputed ×3. `historical` (deep past Targaryens) ≠ `former` (recently-deposed); kept distinct per wiki convention. `claimed` covers all pretender/self-styled cases. | Wiki infobox (strongest signal) |
21	| `VOWS_TO` | `active`, `kept`, `broken`, `fulfilled`, `unknown` | Distinct lifecycle states of a personal oath. Corpus: vow ×369, broken vow ×13, fulfilled vow ×2, swore ×100. `active` is in-force (default); `kept` = honored over years; `broken` = violated; `fulfilled` = completed (Brienne returning Sansa). High narrative weight in ASOIAF (Brienne, Jaime, Arya, Sandor). | Pass 1 corpus |
22	| `MANIPULATES` | `via_bribe`, `via_flattery`, `via_false_information`, `via_threat`, `via_seduction`, `unknown` | Locked Session 55 — confirmed. Original mechanism enum that motivated the whole qualifier-vocab project. | Session 55 verdict |
23	| `SWORN_TO` | `current`, `former`, `deserted`, `by_marriage`, `claimed`, `unknown` | High-volume feudal allegiance type with rich qualifier surface in wiki. Wiki: formerly ×45, in death ×11, possibly ×9, deserted ×3, by marriage ×2, claimed ×2, annulled ×2. `deserted` captures Night's Watch oath-breakers; `by_marriage` captures fealty-via-spouse (Tully→Stark via Catelyn); `claimed` covers contested vassalage. | Wiki infobox |
24	
25	---
26	
27	## Tier 2 — OPTIONAL qualifier (9 edge types)
28	
29	| Edge Type | Enum Values | Rationale | Data Source |
30	|-----------|-------------|-----------|-------------|
31	| `BETROTHED_TO` | `current`, `broken`, `fulfilled`, `secret`, `unknown` | Most betrothal-text is straightforward state-declaration (no qualifier needed). Optional to capture when narratively important. Corpus: betrothed ×335, broke betroth ×3. Pass 1 also showed `secret` betrothals (Robb-Jeyne-Westerling-style). | Pass 1 corpus + series knowledge |
32	| `LOVER_OF` | `current`, `former`, `secret`, `paramour`, `rumored`, `unknown` | Pass 1: former ×19, secret ×7, paramour ×7 in the relation column. Wiki: rumored ×36 (dominant), paramour ×4. `paramour` is the formal Westerosi term for an acknowledged extramarital partner (Ellaria, Bellegere); `rumored` covers the gossip-historical layer (Lyonel Strong / Rhaenyra). | Pass 1 corpus + wiki infobox |
33	| `KILLS` | `in_combat`, `in_duel`, `by_arrow`, `by_blade`, `by_ambush`, `by_proxy`, `by_creature`, `unknown` | Method matters narratively. Corpus: in combat ×117, in a duel ×4, ambush ×188, by proxy ×7, via creature ×14, beheaded ×290, stabbed ×147. `POISONS` is a separate edge type — do NOT fold into KILLS method. `by_creature` covers dragon-burning, wolf-killing, eagle-attack-causing-death. `by_proxy` covers catspaw-style indirect killing. | Pass 1 corpus full-text |
34	| `CONTRACTED_WITH` | `assassination`, `mercenary_service`, `ransom`, `safe_passage`, `construction`, `marriage_brokerage`, `espionage`, `unknown` | Service-type matters. Corpus: hired ×93, sellsword/mercenary ×398, assassinate ×160, ransom ×132, safe passage ×27. The Faceless Men, Golden Company, smaller free companies, ransom negotiations (Tyrion/Lannister deals), Tycho Nestoris's loans — distinct service categories. | Pass 1 corpus |
35	| `DECEIVES` | `by_lie`, `by_disguise`, `by_omission`, `by_false_witness`, `by_silence`, `unknown` | Method matters. Corpus: lie ×647, disguise ×208, false witness ×5. `by_omission` and `by_silence` are distinct narrative devices (Ned's silence on Jon's parentage). | Pass 1 corpus |
36	| `REVEALS_TO` | `voluntary`, `coerced`, `accidental`, `under_torture`, `unknown` | Disclosure conditions matter for trust/credibility scoring. Corpus: voluntarily ×25, under torture ×57, let slip ×10. | Pass 1 corpus |
37	| `ATTACKS` | `in_anger`, `unprovoked`, `in_self_defense`, `on_command`, `by_creature`, `unknown` | Motive context. Corpus: in anger ×23, on command ×19, self-defense ×2, unprovoked ×0 (concept exists; word doesn't). `by_creature` for direwolf/eagle/dragon attacks. Weaker empirical signal than other Tier-2 candidates — could drop to Tier-3 if Haiku smoke reveals it's never emitted. | Pass 1 corpus |
38	| `GUEST_OF` | `shelter`, `feast`, `bread_and_salt`, `safe_conduct`, `gift_exchange`, `refused`, `unknown` | Pass 1 explicitly types hospitality events. 680 rows across 344 chapters; top categories: shelter_offered ×235, feast_given ×85, gift_exchange ×46, safe_conduct ×27, bread_and_salt ×10. `bread_and_salt` is the formal compact (sacred); `shelter` is the common case; `refused` covers shelter_denied / refusal_to_host. Violations are captured by the separate `VIOLATES_GUEST_RIGHT` edge — do not fold into GUEST_OF qualifier. Hostage situations are captured by `PRISONER_OF`, not GUEST_OF. | Pass 1 `## Hospitality & Guest Right` table |
39	| `IN_LAW_OF` | `by_marriage_of_self`, `by_marriage_of_child`, `by_marriage_of_sibling`, `by_marriage_of_parent`, `unknown` | Marriage-affinity relationship. Symmetric. Sonnet's freeform-qualifier corpus has 97 mentions of "good-mother", "good-father", "good-sister", "good-son", "mother-in-law", "sister-in-law" — mostly in Reach noble-house arcs. Qualifier identifies *which marriage* created the affinity: `by_marriage_of_self` (you married their relative), `by_marriage_of_child` (your child married them or their relative), `by_marriage_of_sibling`, `by_marriage_of_parent`. The graph currently forces a two-hop traversal (SPOUSE_OF + PARENT_OF + invert) and gets nothing for cases where only the in-law link is known; `IN_LAW_OF` closes that gap. | Pass 1 corpus (3 P1 rows) + Sonnet freeform qualifier usage (97 mentions) |
40	
41	---
42	
43	## Tier 3 — NO qualifier (default for all other edge types)
44	
45	All edge types NOT listed in Tier 1 or Tier 2 above are Tier 3. These edges emit no `qualifier` field and no `notes` field. The edge stands on its own: `source / edge_type / target / confidence_tier / evidence_snippet / evidence_kind`.
46	
47	**Count check:** Tier 1 (8) + Tier 2 (9) + Tier 3 (~146) = ~163 total (matches architecture.md master vocab count as of Session 63, 2026-05-21 — after KNOWS deprecation).
48	
49	---
50	
51	## Validator rules (for HAIKU-CUTOVER STEP 3)
52	
53	When `scripts/wiki-pass2-validate-edge-jsonl.py` is extended with qualifier enforcement (STEP 3), it must implement:
54	
55	1. Load this file and parse the per-edge-type enum table into a `QUALIFIER_ENUM: {edge_type: (tier, frozenset(enum_values))}` lookup.
56	2. For each `emit_edge` row:
57	   - If `edge_type` is Tier 1: reject if `qualifier` is missing or not in enum.
58	   - If `edge_type` is Tier 2: accept if `qualifier` is absent; reject if present and not in enum.
59	   - If `edge_type` is Tier 3 (not listed above): reject if `qualifier` is present (any value).
60	   - Reject if `notes` field is present on any edge (field deleted from schema 2026-05-18).
61

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""
3	stage4-classify-manderly.py
4	
5	Classifies enriched prose-edge candidates for the characters-house-manderly bucket
6	into emit_edge or reject_just_mention decisions.
7	
8	Input files (from prose-edge-candidates-enriched/):
9	  - theomore-manderly.candidates.jsonl
10	  - therry.candidates.jsonl
11	  - warrick-manderly.candidates.jsonl
12	  - wendel-manderly.candidates.jsonl
13	  - wylis-manderly.candidates.jsonl
14	
15	Output files (to prose-edges-haiku/):
16	  - <slug>.edges.jsonl
17	
18	Decision logic:
19	  - emit_edge: evidence explicitly states a typed relationship between source and target
20	  - reject_just_mention: target mentioned for context only, or relationship cannot be
21	    expressed in the locked vocabulary
22	
23	Key rules enforced:
24	  - valid_edge_types per row is the permissible set; only those types may be emitted
25	  - ENCOUNTERS requires staging_verbs_present to be non-empty (verb gate)
26	  - KNOWS is deprecated; apparent KNOWS -> knows-deprecated-defer-to-pass1
27	  - Tier-1 edges (SIBLING_OF, SPOUSE_OF, PARENT_OF, WARD_OF, HOLDS_TITLE, VOWS_TO,
28	    MANIPULATES, SWORN_TO) REQUIRE a qualifier field
29	  - Tier-3 edges MUST NOT have a qualifier field
30	  - confidence_tier is always 1 for wiki prose
31	  - evidence_kind is always "wiki-entity"
32	  - evidence_snippet is verbatim from evidence_paragraph, <= 200 chars
33	  - The source character is the owner of the wiki page being classified;
34	    evidence_paragraph describes events in that character's biography.
35	    Relationship checkers MUST verify the relationship is between source and target,
36	    not between two third parties mentioned in the same paragraph.
37	"""
38	
39	import json
40	import argparse
41	import sys
42	from pathlib import Path
43	
44	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
45	# Constants
46	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
47	
48	BUCKET = "characters-house-manderly"
49	BUCKET_DIR = Path(
50	    "/Users/mnoth/source/asoiaf-chat/working/wiki/pass2-buckets"
51	) / BUCKET
52	
53	INPUT_DIR = BUCKET_DIR / "prose-edge-candidates-enriched"
54	OUTPUT_DIR = BUCKET_DIR / "prose-edges-haiku"
55	
56	TARGET_FILES = [
57	    "theomore-manderly.candidates.jsonl",
58	    "therry.candidates.jsonl",
59	    "warrick-manderly.candidates.jsonl",
60	    "wendel-manderly.candidates.jsonl",
61	    "wylis-manderly.candidates.jsonl",
62	]
63	
64	# Tier-1 edges: qualifier REQUIRED
65	TIER1_EDGES = frozenset({
66	    "SIBLING_OF",
67	    "SPOUSE_OF",
68	    "PARENT_OF",
69	    "WARD_OF",
70	    "HOLDS_TITLE",
71	    "VOWS_TO",
72	    "MANIPULATES",
73	    "SWORN_TO",
74	})
75	
76	CHARACTER_TYPES = frozenset({
77	    "character.human",
78	    "character.direwolf",
79	    "character.dragon",
80	})
81	
82	EVENT_TYPES = frozenset({
83	    "event.battle",
84	    "event.war",
85	    "event.tournament",
86	})
87	
88	LOCATION_TYPES = frozenset({
89	    "place.location",
90	    "place.region",
91	})
92	
93	ORG_TYPES = frozenset({
94	    "organization.house",
95	    "organization.faction",
96	    "organization.religion",
97	})
98	
99	TITLE_TYPE = "title"
100	CONCEPT_CUSTOM_TYPE = "concept.custom"
101	SPECIES_TYPE = "species"
102	MATERIAL_TYPE = "object.material"
103	FOOD_TYPE = "object.food"
104	CONCEPT_MAGIC_TYPE = "concept.magic"
105	MEDICAL_TYPE = "concept.medical"
106	CONCEPT_TYPE = "concept.culture"
107	
108	
109	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
110	# Output helpers
111	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
112	
113	def make_snippet(paragraph: str) -> str:
114	    """Return the first 200 characters of the evidence paragraph."""
115	    return paragraph[:200]
116	
117	
118	def reject(source_slug: str, target_slug: str, reason: str) -> dict:
119	    return {
120	        "decision": "reject_just_mention",
121	        "candidate_kind": "source_target",
122	        "source_slug": source_slug,
123	        "target_slug": target_slug,
124	        "reason": reason,
125	    }
126	
127	
128	def emit(
129	    source_slug: str,
130	    target_slug: str,
131	    edge_type: str,
132	    evidence_paragraph: str,
133	    source_section: str,
134	    qualifier: str | None = None,
135	) -> dict:
136	    row = {
137	        "decision": "emit_edge",
138	        "candidate_kind": "source_target",
139	        "evidence_kind": "wiki-entity",
140	        "source_slug": source_slug,
141	        "target_slug": target_slug,
142	        "edge_type": edge_type,
143	        "evidence_snippet": make_snippet(evidence_paragraph),
144	        "evidence_section": source_section,
145	        "confidence_tier": 1,
146	    }
147	    if qualifier is not None:
148	        row["qualifier"] = qualifier
149	    return row
150	
151	
152	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
153	# Name extraction helpers
154	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
155	
156	def source_first_name(source_slug: str) -> str:
157	    """e.g. 'wylis-manderly' -> 'wylis'"""
158	    return source_slug.split("-")[0].lower()
159	
160	
161	def target_first_name(target_slug: str) -> str:
162	    """e.g. 'tywin-lannister' -> 'tywin'"""
163	    return target_slug.split("-")[0].lower()
164	
165	
166	def target_display_name(target_slug: str) -> str:
167	    """e.g. 'house-frey' -> 'frey', 'robb-stark' -> 'robb stark'"""
168	    parts = target_slug.split("-")
169	    # Strip leading "house-" etc.
170	    if parts[0] in ("house", "ser", "lord"):
171	        parts = parts[1:]
172	    return " ".join(parts).lower()
173	
174	
175	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
176	# Core classification dispatcher
177	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
178	
179	def classify(row: dict) -> dict:
180	    """Classify a single candidate row. Returns a decision dict."""
181	    source = row["source_slug"]
182	    target = row["target_slug"]
183	    evidence = row.get("evidence_paragraph", "")
184	    target_type = row.get("target_type", "")
185	    valid_types = set(row.get("valid_edge_types", []))
186	    staging_verbs = row.get("staging_verbs_present", [])
187	    section = row.get("source_section", "")
188	    ev = evidence.lower()
189	
190	    sfn = source_first_name(source)  # e.g. "wylis"
191	    tfn = target_first_name(target)  # e.g. "tywin"
192	
193	    def can(etype: str) -> bool:
194	        return etype in valid_types
195	
196	    def e(edge_type: str, qualifier: str | None = None) -> dict:
197	        return emit(source, target, edge_type, evidence, section, qualifier)
198	
199	    def r(reason: str) -> dict:
200	        return reject(source, target, reason)
201	
202	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
203	    # DEPRECATED: KNOWS
204	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
205	    # No KNOWS edge type exists. If something would have been KNOWS, reject.
206	
207	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
208	    # REJECT BY TARGET TYPE: settings / objects / materials / food / custom
209	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
210	    if target_type in (MATERIAL_TYPE, FOOD_TYPE, SPECIES_TYPE):
211	        return r("material/food/species mentioned in narrative setting")
212	
213	    if target_type == CONCEPT_CUSTOM_TYPE:
214	        # fosterage concept: check if source was fostered / fostered someone
215	        if target == "fosterage" and can("WARD_OF"):
216	            if f"fostered" in ev and sfn in ev:
217	                return r("fosterage custom mentioned in context of others, not source directly")
218	        return r("custom/concept mentioned in narrative setting")
219	
220	    if target_type in ("concept.culture", "concept.language"):
221	        return r("culture/language concept mentioned in narrative setting")
222	
223	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
224	    # LOCATIONS
225	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
226	    if target_type in LOCATION_TYPES:
227	        # BORN_AT
228	        if can("BORN_AT"):
229	            if any(p in ev for p in [f"{sfn} was born", f"{sfn} born at", f"{sfn} born in"]):
230	                if tfn in ev or target_display_name(target) in ev:
231	                    return e("BORN_AT")
232	        # DIED_AT
233	        if can("DIED_AT"):
234	            if any(p in ev for p in [f"{sfn} died at", f"{sfn} died in", f"{sfn}'s death at"]):
235	                if tfn in ev or target_display_name(target) in ev:
236	                    return e("DIED_AT")
237	        # IMPRISONED_AT
238	        if can("IMPRISONED_AT"):
239	            loc = target_display_name(target)
240	            if (f"{sfn}" in ev and loc in ev and
241	                    any(p in ev for p in [
242	                        "held captive at", "held captive in", "prisoner at",
243	                        "imprisoned at", "captive at", "captivity in"
244	                    ])):
245	                return e("IMPRISONED_AT")
246	        # BURIED_AT
247	        if can("BURIED_AT"):
248	            loc = target_display_name(target)
249	            if f"{sfn}" in ev and loc in ev and any(p in ev for p in [
250	                "interred in", "buried at", "buried in", "remains are interred",
251	                "bones are interred", "bones are returned",
252	                f"{sfn}'s bones", f"{sfn}'s remains",
253	            ]):
254	                return e("BURIED_AT")
255	        return r("location mentioned in narrative setting")
256	
257	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
258	    # MEDICAL
259	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
260	    if target_type == MEDICAL_TYPE:
261	        if can("DIED_OF"):
262	            if f"{sfn}" in ev and any(p in ev for p in ["died of", "death from", "died from"]):
263	                return e("DIED_OF")
264	        if can("AFFLICTED_BY"):
265	            if f"{sfn}" in ev and any(p in ev for p in ["suffered", "afflicted", "stricken"]):
266	                return e("AFFLICTED_BY")
267	        return r("medical concept mentioned without AFFLICTED_BY/DIED_OF signal")
268	
269	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
270	    # CONCEPT.MAGIC
271	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
272	    if target_type == CONCEPT_MAGIC_TYPE:
273	        return r("magic concept mentioned in narrative context")
274	
275	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
276	    # TITLES
277	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
278	    if target_type == TITLE_TYPE:
279	        if can("HOLDS_TITLE"):
280	            # Source must be directly described as holding this title
281	            if _source_holds_title(ev, sfn, target):
282	                return e("HOLDS_TITLE", qualifier="current")
283	        return r("title mentioned in narrative context; source does not hold it directly")
284	
285	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
286	    # CHARACTERS
287	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
288	    if target_type in CHARACTER_TYPES:
289	        return _classify_character_target(row, source, target, sfn, tfn, ev, valid_types, can, e, r, staging_verbs, section, evidence)
290	
291	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
292	    # EVENTS
293	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
294	    if target_type in EVENT_TYPES:
295	        return _classify_event_target(row, source, target, sfn, tfn, ev, valid_types, can, e, r)
296	
297	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
298	    # ORGANIZATIONS (houses, factions, religions)
299	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
300	    if target_type in ORG_TYPES:
301	        return _classify_org_target(row, source, target, sfn, tfn, ev, valid_types, can, e, r)
302	
303	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
304	    # Fallback
305	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
306	    return r("no-fitting-type-vocab-locked")
307	
308	
309	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
310	# HOLDS_TITLE checker
311	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
312	
313	def _source_holds_title(ev: str, sfn: str, target_slug: str) -> bool:
314	    """
315	    Return True ONLY if the evidence explicitly describes the SOURCE as holding
316	    the named title.
317	
318	    Strict rules:
319	    - Source must be the grammatical subject of the title-attribution.
320	    - Do NOT fire if title is attributed to third parties (e.g., "his household knights",
321	      "Robb is King in the North", "Lord Walder Frey").
322	    - Do NOT fire for compound titles held by others (e.g., Theomore has household knights,
323	      not IS a household knight).
324	    """
325	    target_lower = target_slug.lower().replace("-", " ")
326	
327	    # Reject possessive patterns like "source's [title]" — source has subordinates
328	    # with that title, doesn't hold it themselves
329	    if f"{sfn}'s {target_lower}" in ev or f"lord {sfn}'s {target_lower}" in ev:
330	        return False
331	
332	    # Pattern A: "[source] is/was a/the [title]" — direct attribution
333	    for p in [
334	        f"{sfn} is a {target_lower}", f"{sfn} was a {target_lower}",
335	        f"{sfn} is the {target_lower}", f"{sfn} was the {target_lower}",
336	        f"{sfn} is an {target_lower}", f"{sfn} was an {target_lower}",
337	    ]:
338	        if p in ev:
339	            return True
340	
341	    # Pattern B: "The [title] is/was/wields/acts..." in a sentence where ONLY source is named
342	    # (i.e., "the knight" is a referring expression for source)
343	    referring_patterns = [
344	        f"the {target_lower} is", f"the {target_lower} was",
345	        f"the {target_lower} wields", f"the {target_lower} leads",
346	        f"the {target_lower} acts", f"the {target_lower} rides",
347	        f"the unwed {target_lower}", f"the skilled {target_lower}",
348	        f"a canny {target_lower}", f"a skilled {target_lower}",
349	    ]
350	    for p in referring_patterns:
351	        if p not in ev:
352	            continue
353	        sentences = [s.strip() for s in ev.split(".") if s.strip()]
354	        for sent in sentences:
355	            if p not in sent:
356	                continue
357	            # Source must appear in the same sentence
358	            if sfn not in sent:
359	                continue
360	            # Reject if the title is in a possessive of someone else:
361	            # e.g. "[other]'s knight" or "his household knights"
362	            if f"'s {target_lower}" in sent or f"his {target_lower}" in sent or f"her {target_lower}" in sent:
363	                continue
364	            return True
365	
366	    # Pattern C: "ser [source]" or "lord [source]" (only for generic titles knight/lord)
367	    if target_slug == "knight" and f"ser {sfn}" in ev:
368	        return True
369	    if target_slug == "lord" and f"lord {sfn}" in ev:
370	        return True
371	
372	    return False
373	
374	
375	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
376	# Character target classifier
377	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
378	
379	def _classify_character_target(
380	    row, source, target, sfn, tfn, ev, valid_types, can, e, r, staging_verbs, section, evidence
381	) -> dict:
382	    """Classify a candidate where target_type is character.*"""
383	
384	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
385	    # SIBLING_OF - require explicit sibling phrase with BOTH names or
386	    # clear "older/younger brother/sister, Ser/Lord [target_name]" structure
387	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
388	    if can("SIBLING_OF"):
389	        qual = _check_sibling_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn)
390	        if qual:
391	            return e("SIBLING_OF", qualifier=qual)
392	
393	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
394	    # PARENT_OF - source is parent of target
395	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
396	    if can("PARENT_OF"):
397	        qual = _check_parent_of_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn)
398	        if qual:
399	            return e("PARENT_OF", qualifier=qual)
400	
401	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
402	    # SPOUSE_OF - source is married to target
403	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
404	    if can("SPOUSE_OF"):
405	        qual = _check_spouse_of_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn)
406	        if qual:
407	            return e("SPOUSE_OF", qualifier=qual)
408	
409	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
410	    # BETROTHED_TO - source is betrothed to target
411	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
412	    if can("BETROTHED_TO"):
413	        if _check_betrothed_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
414	            return e("BETROTHED_TO")
415	
416	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
417	    # PRISONER_OF - source is prisoner/captive of target
418	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
419	    if can("PRISONER_OF"):
420	        if _check_prisoner_of_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
421	            return e("PRISONER_OF")
422	
423	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
424	    # PRISONER_EXCHANGE_FOR - source and target are exchanged as prisoners
425	    # Both must be named as the items being exchanged
426	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
427	    if can("PRISONER_EXCHANGE_FOR"):
428	        if _check_prisoner_exchange_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
429	            return e("PRISONER_EXCHANGE_FOR")
430	
431	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
432	    # CAPTURES - source captures target in combat/battle
433	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
434	    if can("CAPTURES"):
435	        if _check_captures_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
436	            return e("CAPTURES")
437	
438	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
439	    # KILLS / KILLED_BY
440	    # Require: explicit "source kills/killed/slew target" pattern
441	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
442	    if can("KILLS"):
443	        if _check_kills_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
444	            return e("KILLS")
445	
446	    # KILLED_BY is inverse; source was killed by target
447	    if can("KILLED_BY"):
448	        if _check_killed_by_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
449	            return e("KILLED_BY")
450	
451	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
452	    # SWORN_TO (character level — sworn to a person)
453	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
454	    if can("SWORN_TO"):
455	        if _check_sworn_to_person_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
456	            return e("SWORN_TO", qualifier="current")
457	
458	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
459	    # SERVES - source serves target
460	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
461	    if can("SERVES"):
462	        if _check_serves_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
463	            return e("SERVES")
464	
465	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
466	    # COMMANDS - source commands target (person)
467	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
468	    if can("COMMANDS"):
469	        if _check_commands_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
470	            return e("COMMANDS")
471	
472	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
473	    # ADVISES - source advises target
474	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
475	    if can("ADVISES"):
476	        if _check_advises_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
477	            return e("ADVISES")
478	
479	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
480	    # BETRAYS - source betrays target
481	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
482	    if can("BETRAYS"):
483	        if _check_betrays_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
484	            return e("BETRAYS")
485	
486	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
487	    # MOURNS - source grieves for/over target
488	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
489	    if can("MOURNS"):
490	        if _check_mourns_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
491	            return e("MOURNS")
492	
493	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
494	    # LOVES - source loves target
495	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
496	    if can("LOVES"):
497	        if _check_loves_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
498	            return e("LOVES")
499	
500	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
501	    # RESPECTS / TRUSTS / DISTRUSTS / FEARS / HATES
502	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
503	    if can("RESPECTS"):
504	        if f"{sfn} respect" in ev and tfn in ev:
505	            return e("RESPECTS")
506	    if can("TRUSTS"):
507	        if f"{sfn} trust" in ev and tfn in ev:
508	            return e("TRUSTS")
509	    if can("DISTRUSTS"):
510	        if f"{sfn} distrust" in ev and tfn in ev:
511	            return e("DISTRUSTS")
512	    if can("FEARS"):
513	        if f"{sfn} fear" in ev and tfn in ev:
514	            return e("FEARS")
515	    if can("HATES"):
516	        if f"{sfn} hates {tfn}" in ev or f"{sfn} hated {tfn}" in ev:
517	            return e("HATES")
518	
519	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
520	    # OPPOSES
521	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
522	    if can("OPPOSES"):
523	        if _check_opposes_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
524	            return e("OPPOSES")
525	
526	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
527	    # DEFEATS
528	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
529	    if can("DEFEATS"):
530	        if _check_defeats_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
531	            return e("DEFEATS")
532	
533	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
534	    # NEGOTIATES_WITH - source directly negotiates with target
535	    # (NOT: third party negotiates with someone that is not the source)
536	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
537	    if can("NEGOTIATES_WITH"):
538	        if _check_negotiates_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
539	            return e("NEGOTIATES_WITH")
540	
541	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
542	    # CONSPIRES_WITH
543	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
544	    if can("CONSPIRES_WITH"):
545	        if f"conspire" in ev and sfn in ev and tfn in ev:
546	            return e("CONSPIRES_WITH")
547	
548	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
549	    # ALLIES_WITH
550	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
551	    if can("ALLIES_WITH"):
552	        if _check_allies_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
553	            return e("ALLIES_WITH")
554	
555	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
556	    # UNCLE_OF / NEPHEW_OF / COUSIN_OF
557	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
558	    if can("UNCLE_OF"):
559	        if _check_uncle_of_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
560	            return e("UNCLE_OF")
561	    if can("NEPHEW_OF"):
562	        if _check_nephew_of_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
563	            return e("NEPHEW_OF")
564	    if can("COUSIN_OF"):
565	        if f"{sfn}'s cousin" in ev and tfn in ev:
566	            return e("COUSIN_OF")
567	        if f"{tfn}'s cousin" in ev and sfn in ev:
568	            return e("COUSIN_OF")
569	
570	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
571	    # WARD_OF - source is ward/foster-child of target
572	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
573	    if can("WARD_OF"):
574	        if _check_ward_of_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
575	            return e("WARD_OF", qualifier="formal")
576	
577	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
578	    # MARRIES_OFF - source arranges marriage for target (or source is married off)
579	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
580	    if can("MARRIES_OFF"):
581	        if _check_marries_off_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
582	            return e("MARRIES_OFF")
583	
584	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
585	    # COURTS
586	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
587	    if can("COURTS"):
588	        if f"{sfn}" in ev and f"{tfn}" in ev and "suitor" in ev:
589	            return e("COURTS")
590	        if f"{sfn} courts {tfn}" in ev:
591	            return e("COURTS")
592	
593	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
594	    # DECEIVES / MANIPULATES
595	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
596	    if can("DECEIVES"):
597	        if f"{sfn} deceiv" in ev and tfn in ev:
598	            return e("DECEIVES")
599	    if can("MANIPULATES"):
600	        if f"{sfn} manipulat" in ev and tfn in ev:
601	            return e("MANIPULATES", qualifier="unknown")
602	
603	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
604	    # RESCUES / HEALS / PROTECTS
605	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
606	    if can("RESCUES"):
607	        if _check_rescues_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
608	            return e("RESCUES")
609	    if can("HEALS"):
610	        if f"{sfn} heal" in ev and tfn in ev:
611	            return e("HEALS")
612	    if can("PROTECTS"):
613	        if f"{sfn} protect" in ev and tfn in ev:
614	            return e("PROTECTS")
615	
616	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
617	    # REVEALS_TO / CONFIDES
618	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
619	    if can("REVEALS_TO"):
620	        if _check_reveals_to_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
621	            return e("REVEALS_TO")
622	
623	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
624	    # ATTENDS - source attends where target is host or target is another attendee
625	    # Only emit if it's an ATTENDS relationship (source→event, not source→person)
626	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
627	    # (for character→character, ATTENDS doesn't make sense; skip)
628	
629	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
630	    # TRAVELS_WITH - source travels in same company as target
631	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
632	    if can("TRAVELS_WITH"):
633	        if _check_travels_with_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
634	            return e("TRAVELS_WITH")
635	
636	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
637	    # COMPANION_OF
638	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
639	    if can("COMPANION_OF"):
640	        if _check_companion_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
641	            return e("COMPANION_OF")
642	
643	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
644	    # ENCOUNTERS - verb gate: staging_verbs_present must be non-empty
645	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
646	    if can("ENCOUNTERS") and staging_verbs:
647	        if _check_encounters_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
648	            return e("ENCOUNTERS")
649	
650	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
651	    # No fitting edge found
652	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
653	    return r("character co-present or mentioned in context without clear typed relationship")
654

656	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
657	# Event target classifier
658	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
659	
660	def _classify_event_target(
661	    row, source, target, sfn, tfn, ev, valid_types, can, e, r
662	) -> dict:
663	    event_name = target.replace("-", " ").lower()
664	
665	    # FIGHTS_IN: source fought in this event
666	    if can("FIGHTS_IN"):
667	        if _check_fights_in_explicit(ev, sfn, event_name):
668	            return e("FIGHTS_IN")
669	
670	    # COMMANDS_IN: source held command role in this event
671	    if can("COMMANDS_IN"):
672	        if _check_commands_in_explicit(ev, sfn, event_name):
673	            return e("COMMANDS_IN")
674	
675	    # PARTICIPATES_IN: source had non-combat involvement (organizer, escort, etc.)
676	    if can("PARTICIPATES_IN"):
677	        if _check_participates_in_explicit(ev, sfn, event_name):
678	            return e("PARTICIPATES_IN")
679	
680	    # ATTENDS: source was present as guest/witness
681	    if can("ATTENDS"):
682	        if _check_attends_explicit(ev, sfn, event_name):
683	            return e("ATTENDS")
684	
685	    # OFFICIATES: source performs ritual/ceremonial role
686	    if can("OFFICIATES"):
687	        if f"{sfn} officiat" in ev and event_name in ev:
688	            return e("OFFICIATES")
689	
690	    return r("event mentioned in narrative context without source participation signal")
691

693	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
694	# Organization target classifier
695	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
696	
697	def _classify_org_target(
698	    row, source, target, sfn, tfn, ev, valid_types, can, e, r
699	) -> dict:
700	    org_name = target.replace("-", " ").lower().replace("house ", "")
701	    full_org_name = target.replace("-", " ").lower()
702	
703	    # SWORN_TO: source is feudally sworn to this org/house
704	    if can("SWORN_TO"):
705	        if _check_sworn_to_org_explicit(ev, sfn, org_name, full_org_name):
706	            return e("SWORN_TO", qualifier="current")
707	
708	    # MEMBER_OF: source belongs to this faction/order
709	    if can("MEMBER_OF"):
710	        if f"{sfn} joined" in ev and org_name in ev:
711	            return e("MEMBER_OF")
712	        if f"{sfn} is a member of" in ev and org_name in ev:
713	            return e("MEMBER_OF")
714	
715	    # OPPOSES: source opposed this org
716	    if can("OPPOSES"):
717	        if _check_opposes_explicit(ev, sfn, org_name):
718	            return e("OPPOSES")
719	
720	    # BETRAYS: source betrayed this org
721	    if can("BETRAYS"):
722	        if f"{sfn} betray" in ev and org_name in ev:
723	            return e("BETRAYS")
724	
725	    # CONSPIRES_WITH
726	    if can("CONSPIRES_WITH"):
727	        if "conspire" in ev and sfn in ev and org_name in ev:
728	            return e("CONSPIRES_WITH")
729	
730	    # ALLIES_WITH
731	    if can("ALLIES_WITH"):
732	        if _check_allies_explicit(ev, sfn, org_name):
733	            return e("ALLIES_WITH")
734	
735	    return r("organization mentioned in narrative context without typed relationship")
736	
737	
738	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
739	# Explicit relationship checkers (strict)
740	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
741	
742	def _check_sibling_explicit(ev: str, sfn: str, tfn: str) -> str | None:
743	    """
744	    Return qualifier ('full', 'half') if evidence explicitly states source and target
745	    are siblings. Returns None otherwise.
746	
747	    Strict: requires an explicit sibling phrase that names BOTH source and target
748	    in a way that unambiguously links them as siblings — not siblings of a third party.
749	
750	    Patterns accepted:
751	      A) "[source], ... [sibling_phrase], [target]" — source is described, target is their sibling
752	      B) "[target], ... [sibling_phrase], [source]" — target described, source is their sibling
753	      C) "his/her [sibling_phrase], Ser/Lord [target]" in context where source is the subject
754	
755	    NOT accepted:
756	      - "accompanied his brother, Ser [other], to X when [target] calls banners"
757	        (the sibling phrase refers to source→other, not source→target)
758	    """
759	    # Build sibling phrase → qualifier map
760	    sibling_phrases = {
761	        "older brother": "full",
762	        "younger brother": "full",
763	        "older sister": "full",
764	        "younger sister": "full",
765	        "half-brother": "half",
766	        "half brother": "half",
767	        "half-sister": "half",
768	        "half sister": "half",
769	    }
770	
771	    for phrase, qual in sibling_phrases.items():
772	        if phrase not in ev:
773	            continue
774	        if tfn not in ev:
775	            continue
776	
777	        # Find sentences containing this phrase and tfn together
778	        sentences = [s.strip() for s in ev.split(".") if s.strip()]
779	        for sent in sentences:
780	            if phrase not in sent or tfn not in sent:
781	                continue
782	            # The phrase and target are in the same sentence.
783	            # Now check: does the phrase directly precede or refer to tfn?
784	            # Pattern: "... [phrase], Ser/Lord/[tfn]" or "[phrase] [tfn]"
785	            # Accept if tfn appears immediately after the phrase (within a few words)
786	            idx_phrase = sent.find(phrase)
787	            idx_tfn = sent.find(tfn)
788	            if idx_phrase < 0 or idx_tfn < 0:
789	                continue
790	            gap_text = sent[idx_phrase + len(phrase): idx_tfn].strip()
791	            # Gap should be short (just "ser", "lord", "," etc.)
792	            gap_words = [w for w in gap_text.replace(",", "").replace("ser", "").replace("lord", "").split() if w]
793	            if len(gap_words) <= 2:
794	                # The phrase directly names the target as the sibling
795	                # Now verify that source is the one who has this sibling,
796	                # NOT a third party. We require sfn to appear in the sentence
797	                # OR for the sentence to begin the paragraph (page subject = source).
798	                if sfn in sent:
799	                    return qual
800	                # If sfn not in this sentence, check if sfn is the page subject
801	                # by verifying sfn appears as the first name in the evidence paragraph.
802	                first_sentence = sentences[0] if sentences else ""
803	                if sfn in first_sentence:
804	                    # Source is the page subject; implicit subject of this sentence
805	                    # BUT we must also check that the sentence doesn't have another
806	                    # character as the possessor of the sibling relation.
807	                    # Reject if the sentence has "[other_name]'s [phrase]" pattern
808	                    possessive_pos = sent.find("'s " + phrase)
809	                    if possessive_pos >= 0:
810	                        # Some other character owns the sibling phrase — not source
811	                        continue
812	                    # Also reject if another character name appears BEFORE the phrase
813	                    # in the sentence and source does NOT appear before the phrase
814	                    before_phrase = sent[:idx_phrase]
815	                    # Only emit if source name is present somewhere nearby
816	                    if sfn in before_phrase:
817	                        return qual
818	
819	    return None
820	
821	
822	def _check_parent_of_explicit(ev: str, sfn: str, tfn: str) -> str | None:
823	    """
824	    Source is parent of target.
825	
826	    Requires: source's son/daughter named as target, OR target's parent named as source.
827	    The target_name (tfn) must be the CHILD whose parent is source, or the specific
828	    named person whose father/mother is source.
829	
830	    NOT accepted: "source's daughter X served Y" where Y is the target (Y is not the child).
831	    """
832	    # "source's son [tfn]" or "source's daughter [tfn]" — tfn must immediately follow
833	    # the possessive phrase (the child's name should be right there, not a distant reference)
834	    for possessive in [f"{sfn}'s son", f"{sfn}'s daughter",
835	                       f"lord {sfn}'s son", f"lord {sfn}'s daughter"]:
836	        if possessive not in ev:
837	            continue
838	        # Find the possessive in the text and check tfn follows IMMEDIATELY (within ~20 chars)
839	        # This ensures "source's daughter Mara" fires for Mara but not for Alysanne
840	        # who appears later in the same sentence

929	def _check_prisoner_of_explicit(ev: str, sfn: str, tfn: str) -> bool:
930	    """
931	    Source is prisoner of target (target is the captor).
932	
933	    Strict: target must be named as the captor/holder, not just co-present.
934	    Pattern: "[source] captured by [target]" or "[target] keeps/held [source] as prisoner"
935	    or "[source] is [target]'s prisoner".
936	    """
937	    # Direct: "source is/was captured by target"
938	    for p in [f"{sfn} is captured by {tfn}", f"{sfn} was captured by {tfn}",
939	              f"{sfn}, captured by {tfn}"]:
940	        if p in ev:
941	            return True
942	
943	    # "in which [source] is captured by [target]"
944	    if f"captured by {tfn}" in ev and sfn in ev:
945	        # Verify source is the one captured (sfn appears before or with the captured phrase)
946	        sentences = [s.strip() for s in ev.split(".") if s.strip()]
947	        for sent in sentences:
948	            if f"captured by {tfn}" in sent and sfn in sent:
949	                return True
950	
951	    # "[target] keeps/kept [source] as prisoner" — target MUST be the actor
952	    for p in [f"{tfn} keeps {sfn}", f"{tfn} kept {sfn}",
953	              f"{tfn} keep {sfn}"]:
954	        if p in ev:
955	            return True
956	    # "keep [source] as prisoner" — only if target appears as the subject in that sentence
957	    for p in [f"keep {sfn} as prisoner", f"kept {sfn} as prisoner"]:
958	        if p in ev:
959	            sentences = [s.strip() for s in ev.split(".") if s.strip()]
960	            for sent in sentences:
961	                if p in sent and tfn in sent:
962	                    # Verify tfn appears BEFORE the action (as subject/agent)
963	                    idx_p = sent.find(p)
964	                    idx_tfn = sent.find(tfn)
965	                    if idx_tfn < idx_p:
966	                        return True
967	
968	    # "held captive at [location]" + target is captor mentioned in same sentence
969	    # This is location-dependent; check carefully
970	    if f"held captive" in ev and sfn in ev and tfn in ev:
971	        sentences = [s.strip() for s in ev.split(".") if s.strip()]
972	        for sent in sentences:
973	            if "held captive" in sent and sfn in sent and tfn in sent:
974	                # Verify target is named as captor (e.g., "held captive at Harrenhal"
975	                # with Tywin present) — but need to check tfn is the captor, not just nearby
976	                # Accept if sentence has "kept by tfn" or "tfn's prisoner" or "held by tfn"
977	                if (f"kept by {tfn}" in sent or f"{tfn}'s prisoner" in sent
978	                        or f"held by {tfn}" in sent or f"captive of {tfn}" in sent):
979	                    return True
980	
981	    return False

984	def _check_prisoner_exchange_explicit(ev: str, sfn: str, tfn: str) -> bool:
985	    """
986	    Source and target are in a formal prisoner exchange (both are the prisoners being swapped).
987	
988	    Pattern: "trade [source] and [target] for [someone]"
989	           or "trade [source] for [target]" (source and target are the exchanged prisoners)
990	
991	    Strict: The target must appear in the trade clause as a PRISONER being exchanged,
992	    not as the proposer (Tyrion), messenger (Cleos), or bystander.
993	    """
994	    sentences = [s.strip() for s in ev.split(".") if s.strip()]
995	    for sent in sentences:
996	        # "trade [sfn] and [tfn] for X" — both source and target are in the trade
997	        if f"trade {sfn} and {tfn}" in sent:
998	            return True
999	        if f"trade {tfn} and {sfn}" in sent:
1000	            return True
1001	        # "trade [sfn] for [tfn]" — source traded for target (target is the other prisoner)
1002	        if f"trade {sfn}" in sent and f"for {tfn}" in sent:
1003	            # Validate structure: "trade X for Y" means X and Y are the prisoners.
1004	            # Reject if tfn appears BEFORE "trade" in the sentence (proposer role).
1005	            idx_trade = sent.find(f"trade {sfn}")
1006	            idx_tfn = sent.find(tfn)
1007	            if idx_tfn > idx_trade:
1008	                return True
1009	        if f"trade {tfn}" in sent and f"for {sfn}" in sent:
1010	            idx_trade = sent.find(f"trade {tfn}")
1011	            idx_sfn = sent.find(sfn)
1012	            if idx_sfn > idx_trade:
1013	                return True
1014	    return False

1360	def _check_participates_in_explicit(ev: str, sfn: str, event_name: str) -> bool:
1361	    """Source had non-combat involvement in the event."""
1362	    if event_name not in ev:
1363	        return False
1364	    # Organizing: "held a tourney", "hosted", "attended" in non-combat context
1365	    organize_verbs = ["held a", "hosted a", "organized", "organized the"]
1366	    for v in organize_verbs:
1367	        sentences = [s.strip() for s in ev.split(".") if s.strip()]
1368	        for sent in sentences:
1369	            if sfn in sent and event_name in sent and v in sent:
1370	                return True
1371	    # Baggage train, logistics
1372	    if "baggage train" in ev and sfn in ev and event_name in ev:
1373	        return True
1374	    return False
1375	
1376	
1377	def _check_attends_explicit(ev: str, sfn: str, event_name: str) -> bool:
1378	    """
1379	    Source attended the event as guest/spectator.
1380	
1381	    Strict: source must be explicitly described as attending/watching the event.
1382	    NOT accepted if the event is merely mentioned in context of someone else's actions.
1383	    """
1384	    if event_name not in ev:
1385	        return False
1386	    sentences = [s.strip() for s in ev.split(".") if s.strip()]
1387	    for sent in sentences:
1388	        if sfn not in sent or event_name not in sent:
1389	            continue
1390	        # Explicit attendance language about source
1391	        if any(p in sent for p in [
1392	            f"{sfn} attended", f"{sfn} attend",
1393	            f"{sfn} spectated", f"{sfn} watched",
1394	            f"{sfn} is entertained by the",
1395	            f"{sfn} was entertained by",
1396	        ]):
1397	            return True
1398	    return False
1399	
1400	
1401	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
1402	# Main processing
1403	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
1404	
1405	def process_file(input_path: Path, output_dir: Path) -> tuple[int, int]:
1406	    """
1407	    Process one candidates file. Returns (emit_count, reject_count).
1408	
1409	    Deduplication: after classification, emitted edges are deduplicated by
1410	    (source_slug, target_slug, edge_type) — keeping the first occurrence.
1411	    Duplicates arise when the same relationship appears in multiple wiki sections
1412	    (e.g., "Narrative Arc / AGOT" and "Quotes by Wylis" both describe the
1413	    same SIBLING_OF relationship).
1414	    """
1415	    slug = input_path.stem.replace(".candidates", "")
1416	    output_path = output_dir / f"{slug}.edges.jsonl"
1417	
1418	    all_decisions: list[dict] = []
1419	    seen_emit_keys: set[tuple] = set()
1420	
1421	    with input_path.open("r", encoding="utf-8") as fin:
1422	        for line_num, line in enumerate(fin, 1):
1423	            line = line.strip()
1424	            if not line:
1425	                continue
1426	
1427	            try:
1428	                row = json.loads(line)
1429	            except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
1430	                print(
1431	                    f"  WARNING: line {line_num} in {input_path.name} invalid JSON: {exc}",
1432	                    file=sys.stderr,
1433	                )
1434	                continue
1435	
1436	            try:
1437	                decision = classify(row)
1438	            except Exception as exc:
1439	                print(
1440	                    f"  WARNING: classification error line {line_num} in {input_path.name}: {exc}",
1441	                    file=sys.stderr,
1442	                )
1443	                src = row.get("source_slug", "unknown")
1444	                tgt = row.get("target_slug", "unknown")
1445	                decision = reject(src, tgt, f"classification-error: {exc}")
1446	
1447	            # Deduplicate emit_edge rows by (source, target, edge_type)
1448	            if decision["decision"] == "emit_edge":
1449	                dedup_key = (
1450	                    decision["source_slug"],
1451	                    decision["target_slug"],
1452	                    decision["edge_type"],
1453	                )
1454	                if dedup_key in seen_emit_keys:
1455	                    # Convert to reject with dedup reason instead of writing duplicate
1456	                    decision = reject(
1457	                        decision["source_slug"],
1458	                        decision["target_slug"],
1459	                        "duplicate-edge-across-sections",
1460	                    )
1461	                else:
1462	                    seen_emit_keys.add(dedup_key)
1463	
1464	            all_decisions.append(decision)
1465	
1466	    emit_count = sum(1 for d in all_decisions if d["decision"] == "emit_edge")
1467	    reject_count = sum(1 for d in all_decisions if d["decision"] != "emit_edge")
1468	
1469	    with output_path.open("w", encoding="utf-8") as fout:
1470	        for decision in all_decisions:
1471	            fout.write(json.dumps(decision, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
1472	
1473	    return emit_count, reject_count
1474	
1475	
1476	def main() -> None:
1477	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
1478	        description="Classify enriched Manderly prose-edge candidates into emit/reject decisions."
1479	    )
1480	    parser.add_argument(
1481	        "--input-dir",
1482	        type=Path,
1483	        default=INPUT_DIR,
1484	        help="Directory containing *.candidates.jsonl files",
1485	    )
1486	    parser.add_argument(
1487	        "--output-dir",
1488	        type=Path,
1489	        default=OUTPUT_DIR,
1490	        help="Directory to write *.edges.jsonl output files",
1491	    )
1492	    parser.add_argument(
1493	        "--files",
1494	        nargs="+",
1495	        default=TARGET_FILES,
1496	        help="Specific candidate filenames to process (default: all 5 Manderly files)",
1497	    )
1498	    args = parser.parse_args()
1499	
1500	    args.output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
1501	
1502	    total_emit = 0
1503	    total_reject = 0
1504	
1505	    for filename in args.files:
1506	        input_path = args.input_dir / filename
1507	        if not input_path.exists():
1508	            print(f"  WARNING: {input_path} not found — skipping", file=sys.stderr)
1509	            continue
1510	
1511	        emit_count, reject_count = process_file(input_path, args.output_dir)
1512	        total_emit += emit_count
1513	        total_reject += reject_count
1514	        print(
1515	            f"[done] {filename} → {emit_count} emit_edge, {reject_count} reject_just_mention"
1516	        )
1517	
1518	    print()
1519	    print(f"Summary: {len(args.files)} files processed")
1520	    print(f"  Total emit_edge:           {total_emit}")
1521	    print(f"  Total reject_just_mention: {total_reject}")
1522	    print(f"  Total rows:                {total_emit + total_reject}")
1523	    print(f"Output written to: {args.output_dir}")
1524	
1525	
1526	if __name__ == "__main__":
1527	    main()
1528

input.new_string:
def _check_commands_in_explicit(ev: str, sfn: str, event_name: str) -> bool:
    """Source commanded in the event.

    Requires that both the event name AND the command-attribution signal appear
    in the same sentence (or at minimum the paragraph), and that source is named
    as the one receiving/executing the command.
    """
    if event_name not in ev:
        return False
    if sfn not in ev:
        return False

    command_signals = [
        f"{sfn} commands", f"{sfn} commanded",
        # "granted command of X army to [source]"
        f"granted command of", f"command to {sfn}",
    ]

    sentences = [s.strip() for s in ev.split(".") if s.strip()]
    for sent in sentences:
        # Both event and source must appear in the same sentence as a command signal
        if event_name not in sent:
            continue
        if sfn not in sent:
            continue
        if any(p in sent for p in command_signals):
            return True

    # Also check cross-sentence: if one sentence has "granted command to [source]"
    # and another has the event name, only emit if they're clearly linked.
    # We require both in the same sentence for safety — skip cross-sentence case.
    return False

tool_use_result.prompt:
Write a Python script `stage4-classify-manderly.py` that reads 5 JSONL candidates files from working/wiki/pass2-buckets/characters-house-manderly/prose-edge-candidates-enriched/ and emits classified edges JSONL to prose-edges-haiku/ subdirectory.

**Files to process (input → output):**
1. theomore-manderly.candidates.jsonl → theomore-manderly.edges.jsonl
2. therry.candidates.jsonl → therry.edges.jsonl
3. warrick-manderly.candidates.jsonl → warrick-manderly.edges.jsonl
4. wendel-manderly.candidates.jsonl → wendel-manderly.edges.jsonl
5. wylis-manderly.candidates.jsonl → wylis-manderly.edges.jsonl

**Classification logic (all candidates are source_target kind):**

For each candidate row, output ONE decision:
1. **emit_edge** if a real relationship exists. Fields:
   - decision, candidate_kind (= "source_target"), evidence_kind (= "wiki-entity"), source_slug, target_slug, edge_type, qualifier (only if Tier-1), evidence_snippet, evidence_section, confidence_tier (integer 1-3)
2. **reject_just_mention** if just a mention, not an edge. Fields:
   - decision, candidate_kind, source_slug, target_slug, reason (string)

**Tier-1 edges requiring qualifier** (from reference/edge-qualifier-vocab.md):
- SIBLING_OF: full|half|step|milk|unknown
- SPOUSE_OF: current|former|annulled|widowed|salt_wife|unknown
- PARENT_OF: biological|adopted|claimed|rumored|disputed|unknown
- WARD_OF: formal|informal|hostage|unknown
- HOLDS_TITLE: current|former|claimed|contested|historical|unknown
- VOWS_TO: active|kept|broken|fulfilled|unknown
- MANIPULATES: via_bribe|via_flattery|via_false_information|via_threat|via_seduction|unknown
- SWORN_TO: current|former|deserted|by_marriage|claimed|unknown

**Classification rules** (apply in order):
1. If evidence_paragraph is empty or "_python_prereject" field exists, reject with appropriate reason
2. If target_type NOT in valid_edge_types, check type contracts OR reject reason `type-contract-violation`
3. If staging_verbs_present is empty and candidate seems like it needs ENCOUNTERS, reject reason `no-staging-verb`
4. For most candidates: emit LOCATED_AT (character/person→place), TRAVELS_TO (person→place), TRAVELS_WITH (person↔person), SIBLING_OF, PARENT_OF, SERVANT_RELATIONSHIP (person→person), OPPOSES, DEFEATS, KILLS, etc. based on prose semantics
5. When in doubt, reject_just_mention with reason `temporal-cooccurrence-not-relational` or `no-fitting-type-vocab-locked`

**Heuristics for the Manderly family:**
- Many candidates link Manderly characters to locations (White Harbor, Northern castles) → LOCATED_AT or reject
- Family relationships (siblings, parents) → detect kinship verbs in evidence_paragraph
- Service relationships → detect SERVES language
- Battle/military participation → PARTICIPATES_IN, FIGHTS_IN toward event targets
- Title holding → HOLDS_TITLE

**Implementation:**
- Read each input JSONL line by line
- For each candidate, apply classification logic using the evidence_paragraph, target_type, and valid_edge_types
- No HTTP calls, all data is local
- Output one JSONL file per input file, same directory structure with prose-edges-haiku/ instead of prose-edge-candidates-enriched/
- Print summary: "[done] filename → N emit_edge, M reject_just_mention"

**Edge type vocabulary** (163 types; use ONLY these):
LOCATED_AT, TRAVELS_TO, TRAVELS_WITH, SIBLING_OF, PARENT_OF, SPOUSE_OF, BETROTHED_TO, SWORN_TO, SERVES, COMMANDS, OPPOSES, FIGHTS_IN, KILLS, DEFEATS, ATTENDS, PARTICIPATES_IN, HOLDS_TITLE, BORN_AT, DIED_AT, BURIED_AT, IMPRISONED_AT, LOCATED_IN (deprecated, use LOCATED_AT), and the full ~163-type vocabulary inlined in the classifier prompt (available in reference/architecture.md).

Use ONLY types present in valid_edge_types for each row. If no fit, reject with reason `no-fitting-type-vocab-locked`.

Be pragmatic: for character-to-place links, LOCATED_AT is usually right. For temporal co-presence without a specific typed edge, reject. For family members in the same region, emit SIBLING_OF if evidence supports it.

content.thinking:
Looking at the output files, I can see there are some **schema and logic issues** that need to be corrected:

**Issue 1: BETROTHED_TO edge on line 21 of theomore-manderly.edges.jsonl**
```json
{"decision": "emit_edge", "candidate_kind": "source_target", "evidence_kind": "wiki-entity", "source_slug": "theomore-manderly", "target_slug": "viserra-targaryen", "edge_type": "BETROTHED_TO", "evidence_snippet": "In «58 AC» Lord Manderly attended a tourney in the capital to celebrate the tenth anniversary of Jaehaerys's coronation. One of Theomore's daughters «married» Lord «Darklyn» at «Duskendale» in «72 AC»", "evidence_section": "## Origins", "confidence_tier": 1}
```

This edge has NO `qualifier` field, but `BETROTHED_TO` is a **Tier-1 edge that REQUIRES a qualifier**. The evidence snippet also shows a daughter marrying, not betrothal. This is wrong.

Also, the edge_type BETROTHED_TO doesn't make sense here. The evidence says "married", not "betrothed". So this should be `SPOUSE_OF` with qualifier `current` or `former`, OR `MARRIES_OFF` (if Theomore arranged the marriage). But actually, since the daughter married Darklyn, the edge should be on the daughter's node, not Theomore's (if it's SPOUSE_OF), or should be MARRIES_OFF if Theomore arranged it.

**Issue 2: PARTICIPATES_IN on line 20 of theomore-manderly.edges.jsonl**
The edge_type is PARTICIPATES_IN but the target is "tourney" — a title, not an event.event.* type. 

PARTICIPATES_IN is a valid edge type, but "tourney" appears to be a title rather than an event node, which could violate the type contract.

**Issue 3: Evidence snippets are truncated**
Looking at the warrick-manderly line 8, the snippet cuts off mid-word: "ending the re" instead of completing "ending the rebellion". These truncations break the readability and usefulness of the evidence.

**Issue 4: SIBLING_OF with proper qualifier**
The wylis-manderly edge correctly includes qualifier "full" for SIBLING_OF, supported by the shared father Lord Wyman mentioned in the evidence.

**Issue 5: Multiple critical violations**
Several problems stand out: BETROTHED_TO edges are missing required qualifiers at Tier-1, evidence snippets remain truncated rather than complete verbatim substrings, and there are potential type-contract violations where "tourney" is used as a target of PARTICIPATES_IN. Additionally, the logical claim that Theomore becomes betrothed to Viserra contradicts the actual source material—his daughter marries Darklyn, not Viserra to Theomore.

These schema violations would be flagged by validation. I should either run the script again with corrections applied, or manually fix these edges before proceeding.

   - Location: theomore-manderly.edges.jsonl line 21
   - Current: `"edge_type": "BETROTHED_TO"` with NO qualifier field
   - Fix: BETROTHED_TO is Tier-1 and REQUIRES `"qualifier": "current|former|annulled|widowed|salt_wife|unknown"`
   - Also: The logic is wrong — evidence says daughter "married" Darklyn, not that Theomore became betrothed. This should either be:
     - Reject (daughter's relationship, not Theomore's)
     - Or emit `MARRIES_OFF` (if evidence shows Theomore arranged the marriage)
     - Or extract from evidence whether the marriage was betrothal or consummated

   - Location: warrick-manderly.edges.jsonl line 8 (COMMANDS_IN sistermens-rebellion)
   - Current: `"evidence_snippet": "...ending the re"` (cut off mid-word)
   - Fix: Must be complete sentence(s) from evidence_paragraph, e.g., "Torrhen granted command of a northern army to Warrick, who sailed from White Harbor to the Bite"

3. **Type-contract and logic issues**

- NEVER omit the field — it's REQUIRED

- Must be complete sentence(s), not truncated mid-word

1	# The Weirwood Network — Schema Reference
2	
3	> **This file is the schema reference for all agents working on this project.** It defines entity types, edge types, confidence tiers, file naming conventions, and spoiler gating. Every agent should load this file to understand the data model.
4	>
5	> For project overview, directory structure, and pipeline sequence, see `CLAUDE.md`.
6	> For current project state and progress, see `worklog.md`.
7	
8	---
9	
10	## File Naming Conventions
11	
12	### Chapter Source Files
13	`{book-abbrev}-{pov-character}-{chapter-number}.md`
14	
15	Book abbreviations: `agot`, `acok`, `asos`, `affc`, `adwd`
16	
17	Novella codes (Tales of Dunk and Egg): `thk` (The Hedge Knight), `tss` (The Sworn Sword), `tmk` (The Mystery Knight)
18	
19	Examples:
20	- `agot-bran-01.md` (Bran's first chapter in AGOT)
21	- `asos-catelyn-07.md` (Catelyn's seventh chapter in ASOS — the Red Wedding)
22	- `affc-prologue.md` (prologues/epilogues use that as the name)
23	
24	D&E novellas use `collection: tales-of-dunk-and-egg` in frontmatter and `first_available: pre-agot` for spoiler gating (set pre-AGOT, ~90 years before the main series).
25	
26	### Extraction Files
27	Same name as source chapter with `.extraction.md` suffix:
28	- `agot-bran-01.extraction.md`
29	
30	### Node Files
31	`{entity-name-kebab-case}.node.md`
32	- `jaqen-hghar.node.md`
33	- `horn-of-joramun.node.md`
34	- `the-citadel.node.md`
35	
36	---
37	
38	## Entity Type Hierarchy
39	
40	Every node in the graph belongs to a type in this hierarchy. Child types inherit from their parent — a query for "organizations" returns houses, factions, and religions. A query for "events" returns both wars and individual battles.
41	
42	This hierarchy is **extensible**. New leaf types can be added without restructuring; new intermediate types should be rare and require design review.
43	
44	```
45	Entity
46	├── Character
47	│   ├── Human
48	│   ├── Direwolf          (Ghost, Grey Wind, Lady, Nymeria, Summer, Shaggydog)
49	│   └── Dragon            (Drogon, Rhaegal, Viserion; also historical: Balerion, Vhagar)
50	├── Place
51	│   ├── Location          (specific: Winterfell, Tower of Joy, The Citadel)
52	│   └── Region            (geographic: The North, Dorne, The Reach, Beyond the Wall)
53	├── Organization
54	│   ├── House             (sigil, words, seat, bloodline: House Stark, House Frey)
55	│   ├── Faction           (Night's Watch, Faceless Men, Golden Company, Brotherhood Without Banners)
56	│   └── Religion          (deities, clergy, rituals, sacred sites: Faith of the Seven, R'hllor, Old Gods)
57	├── Concept
58	│   ├── Culture           (customs, language, appearance norms: Dothraki, Ironborn, Free Folk)
59	│   ├── Magic             (rules, practitioners, costs: warging, greensight, shadowbinding, wildfire)
60	│   ├── Prophecy          (Azor Ahai, Maggy the Frog, House of the Undying visions)
61	│   └── Theory            (R+L=J, Grand Northern Conspiracy, Eldritch Apocalypse)
62	├── Object
63	│   ├── Artifact          (Valyrian steel swords, horns, crowns: Ice, Dragonbinder, Dawn)
64	│   └── Text              (in-world books, letters, songs: The Jade Compendium, The Rains of Castamere)
65	├── Event
66	│   ├── Battle            (single engagement: Red Wedding, Battle of the Blackwater)
67	│   ├── War               (multi-battle conflict: Robert's Rebellion, War of the Five Kings)
68	│   └── Tournament        (formal tourney: Tourney at Harrenhal, Hand's Tourney, Ashford Tourney)
69	├── Species               (biological type, NOT individual: dragons-as-species, Others, Children of the Forest)
70	├── Title                 (formal office: Hand of the King, Lord Commander, High Septon)
71	└── Meta                  (out-of-universe constructs — chapters, novels, publication metadata)
72	    ├── Chapter           (a chapter of a published novel: AGOT Chapter 1, ASOS Chapter 71)
73	    └── (future)          (Book, PovArc — added when needed)
74	```
75	
76	**Meta vs in-world** — Meta entities are out-of-universe (they describe the books *about* the world, not things *in* the world). They are categorically distinct from Event entities (Red Wedding, Battle of the Blackwater), which are in-world happenings. A `meta.chapter` node represents the literary container; the in-world events that happen *within* a chapter remain their own `event.battle/war/tournament` nodes. Edges from in-world entities to `meta.chapter` nodes are *citation/provenance* edges (this entity is featured in / discussed in this chapter), not in-world relationships.
77	
78	### Type Reference Table
79	
80	| Type | Parent | Description | Distinguishing Fields | Examples |
81	|------|--------|-------------|----------------------|----------|
82	| `character.human` | Character | Named human individual | name, aliases, culture, allegiance, born, died | Jon Snow, Jaqen H'ghar, Cersei Lannister |

108	### Hierarchy Query Rules
109	
110	- **Querying a parent returns all children.** "Show me all Organizations" → houses + factions + religions.
111	- **Querying a leaf returns only that type.** "Show me all Houses" → only houses, not factions.
112	- **An entity has exactly one type.** The Night's Watch is `organization.faction`, not both faction and religion. If an entity genuinely straddles types, pick the primary and note the secondary in metadata.
113	- **Characters are individuals, species are categories.** Drogon is `character.dragon`. "Dragons" as a biological category is `species`. Ghost is `character.direwolf`. "Direwolves" as a species is `species`.
114	
115	### Multi-type entities
116	
117	Some real-world entities span multiple of the type categories above. Free Folk are simultaneously a culture (`concept.culture`) and a polity/faction (`organization.faction`). Children of the Forest are simultaneously a species (`species`) and an ancient sentient faction. Wardens are titles, but the *role* of being a warden is also a behavior set.
118	
119	**Policy: one node per real-world entity. The `type` field captures its primary identity. Other facets emerge through edges, not through a second node.**
120	
121	- Free Folk → `concept.culture`. Polity-ness emerges via MEMBER_OF (character → free-folk), FIGHTS_IN (war/battle → free-folk), HOLDS_TITLE (king-beyond-the-wall), LOCATED_AT (beyond-the-wall → free-folk).
122	- Children of the Forest → `species`. Faction-ness emerges via FIGHTS_IN (war-of-first-men), LOCATED_AT (isle-of-faces), and magic-use edges.
123	
124	This avoids SAME_AS bookkeeping and ambiguous "which node?" queries. Retrieval naturally unions identity + behaviors via edge traversal.
125	
126	**The `multi-type-entity-resolver` agent's job** under this policy: pick the right primary type + ensure edges capture the other facets. NOT split into multiple nodes.
127	
128	---
129	
130	## Edge Types (Relationship Categories)
131	
132	Edges connect nodes in the graph. Every edge must have:
133	- `type` — from the taxonomy below
134	- `source` / `target` — the two nodes
135	- `first_available` — spoiler gate
136	- `evidence` — chapter citation or wiki source
137	- `confidence` — tier 1-5
138	
139	### Design Principles
140	
141	The v1 AGOT extractions organically produced ~127 ad-hoc relationship labels. The wiki infobox fields surface another ~40 structured relationship types. This taxonomy normalizes both into a controlled vocabulary that is **specific enough to be queryable** but **general enough to avoid synonyms** (e.g., one `SERVES` rather than SERVES / SERVED_BY / CLAIMS_TO_SERVE / SWORN_TO all meaning slightly different things).
142	
143	When an extraction or wiki field doesn't fit an existing edge type, add a new one to this taxonomy rather than forcing a bad fit. Edge types are cheaper than lost information.
144	
145	**When this taxonomy is used:** This controlled vocabulary is for the **graph layer** (building nodes and edges in `graph/`), the **wiki ingestion pass** (Pass 2), and any **downstream analytical passes**. Pass 1 (mechanical extraction) records relationships in free-text natural language in its Relationships Observed table — it does NOT need to use these labels. The normalization from free-text → controlled vocabulary happens when graph edges are built from extraction outputs.
146	
147	**Qualifier enums for Tier-1/Tier-2 edge types:** see `reference/edge-qualifier-vocab.md`. Eight types require a `qualifier` field (Tier 1: SIBLING_OF, SPOUSE_OF, PARENT_OF, WARD_OF, HOLDS_TITLE, VOWS_TO, MANIPULATES, SWORN_TO); nine types allow an optional qualifier (Tier 2: BETROTHED_TO, LOVER_OF, KILLS, CONTRACTED_WITH, DECEIVES, REVEALS_TO, ATTACKS, GUEST_OF, IN_LAW_OF). All other edge types must NOT carry a `qualifier` field.
148
