155	| `SPOUSE_OF` | Married to (note if current, former, or annulled) | Symmetric | Spouse, Spouses |
156	| `BETROTHED_TO` | Engaged/promised in marriage | Symmetric | — |
157	| `LOVER_OF` | Romantic/sexual relationship outside marriage | Symmetric | Lover, Lovers |
158	| `WARD_OF` | Fostered by / raised by (not biological parent). Reverse-direction `FOSTERED_BY` (Guardian → Ward) is permitted and semantically equivalent — same pattern as HELD_BY for HOLDS_TITLE. | Ward → Guardian | — |
159	| `ANCESTOR_OF` | Distant lineage (more than one generation) | Ancestor → Descendant | Dynasty |
160	| `HEIR_TO` | Designated or expected successor (person → person or person → title) | Heir → Holder | Heir, Heirs |
161	| `CADET_BRANCH_OF` | Junior house derived from senior house | Cadet → Parent House | Cadet branches |
162	| `MARRIES_OFF` | Parent / overlord / king arranges a marriage for another person — distinct from `SPOUSE_OF` (the marriage itself); captures the arranger's agency as a political instrument | Arranger → Married-off person | — |
163	| `UNCLE_OF` | One-generation kinship shortcut: parent's sibling. Captures uncle/aunt without forcing two-hop traversal through the missing/inferred parent. Use when prose explicitly says "his uncle X" / "her aunt Y". | Uncle/Aunt → Nephew/Niece | — |
164	| `NEPHEW_OF` | Reverse of `UNCLE_OF` — emitted on the nephew/niece node pointing to the uncle/aunt. Same one-hop kinship shortcut. | Nephew/Niece → Uncle/Aunt | — |
165	| `COUSIN_OF` | Symmetric kinship shortcut for cousins (children of siblings). Captures first/second/etc. cousins without traversing two PARENT_OF + one SIBLING_OF. Use when prose explicitly says "his cousin X" / "her cousin Y". Especially common in Frey, Lannister, Tully, Tyrell, Targaryen families. | Symmetric | — |
166	| `MILK_BROTHER_OF` | Symmetric kinship: characters who shared a wet-nurse. Real Westerosi cultural category (Edric Dayne and Jon Snow, Robert Baratheon and Ned Stark per fostering customs). Distinct from SIBLING_OF (no blood) and FOSTERED_BY (institutional). | Symmetric | — |
167	| `NURSED_BY` | Child was wet-nursed by this person. Reverse is `WET_NURSE_OF`. Distinct from PARENT_OF; captures the lifelong attachment ASOIAF treats as significant (Wylla nursing Edric, Catelyn re Jon's nurse, etc.). | Child → Nurse | — |
168	| `WET_NURSE_OF` | Reverse of NURSED_BY — emitted on the nurse's node pointing to the child she nursed. | Nurse → Child | — |
169	| `COURTS` | Active suitor relationship — pre-betrothal pursuit of marriage. Distinct from `BETROTHED_TO` (formal engagement) and `LOVER_OF` (sexual/romantic). Use when prose describes someone as a "suitor" / "sought her hand" / "courted." Common pattern: Lysa Arryn's suitors after Jon Arryn's death; Rohanne Webber's suitors; Sansa pre-marriage. | Suitor → Object-of-courtship | — |
170	| `PROPOSED_AS_BRIDE` | A third party proposes a specific woman as a bride for a specific man (or for a Throne-political match). Distinct from `MARRIES_OFF` (the actual arrangement of an executed marriage) and `BETROTHED_TO` (formalized engagement). Captures the diplomatic-offer stage common in Westerosi succession politics. | Proposer → Proposed bride | — |
171	| `STEP_PARENT_OF` | Step-parent to a step-child — a marital-consequence relation distinct from `PARENT_OF` (biological/adopted) and `WARD_OF` (institutional fostering). Use when prose explicitly says "stepmother", "stepfather", or structurally implies a step-parental relationship via a parent's marriage. Reverse is `STEP_CHILD_OF`. | Step-parent → Step-child | n/a (marital-consequence relation) |
172	| `STEP_CHILD_OF` | Reverse of `STEP_PARENT_OF` — emitted on the step-child's node pointing to the step-parent. | Step-child → Step-parent | n/a |
173	| `IN_LAW_OF` | Marriage-affinity relationship between two people connected by the marriage of a third. Symmetric. Covers "good-mother", "good-father", "good-sister", "good-brother", "mother-in-law", "sister-in-law", etc. Distinct from `SPOUSE_OF` (the marriage itself), `SIBLING_OF` (blood/milk/step), `PARENT_OF` (parentage). Use when a two-hop traversal (SPOUSE_OF + PARENT_OF + invert) would lose the named in-law relationship known from prose. Optional qualifier enum: see `reference/edge-qualifier-vocab.md`. | Symmetric | corpus (97 "good-mother/good-father/mother-in-law" mentions) |

Tier-3 (no qualifier; method is sub-typology the narrative captures). | Torturer → Tortured-person | corpus |

Distinct from `ALLIES_WITH` (political alliance), `TRUSTS` (one-direction confidence), `LOVES` (romantic/deep-familial), `RESPECTS` (cold regard). Use when prose explicitly names a friendship ("good friends with", "sworn brothers", "close companion"). Examples: Patrek Mallister & Edmure Tully; Robert & Ned in their youth; Brienne & Pod; Davos & Salladhor Saan. | Symmetric | — |
274	| `REPUTED_AS` | Collective reputation or general perception attached to a character without a specific perceiver — distinct from `PERCEIVED_AS` which requires a named POV. Target is `concept.*` (e.g., `concept.magic` for "reputed witch", `concept.craft` for "reputed swordsman", `concept.vice` for "reputed drunkard"). Use when prose narrates a public-domain reputation rather than one character's view of another. | Character → Concept | — |
275	| `ENCOUNTERS` | Plot-significant face-to-face meeting between two characters, anchored by explicit prose staging (verb gate enforced by validator per CRITICAL RULE — see classify prompt Rule 6). Reserved for first meetings, brief road-crossings, set-piece confrontations short of duel/combat where the meeting itself carries narrative weight. Examples: Dunk ↔ Egg at the Ashford inn; Brienne ↔ Randyll Tarly in AFFC; Arya ↔ Sandor on the kingsroad; Jon ↔ Mance at the Frostfangs; Sam ↔ Coldhands beyond the Wall. NOT for co-presence at events (use `LOCATED_AT` / `ATTENDS` / `FIGHTS_IN`), retinue/court presence (`TRAVELS_WITH`), or formal diplomatic engagement (`NEGOTIATES_WITH`). **Coverage scope (Session 63, 2026-05-21):** Stage 4 captures only wiki-prose-staged meetings — biographical-summary register often elides staging verbs even when meetings happened in-text. Comprehensive character-meeting coverage will come from a future book-derived pass; treat wiki ENCOUNTERS as partial-by-design. Tier-3 (no qualifier). | Symmetric (character ↔ character) | — |

321	### Magic & Supernatural
322	
323	> These edges capture relationships that involve magical, ritual, or supernatural agency — distinct from in-world physical relations. ASOIAF has rich magical systems (warging, R'hllor blood magic, Faceless-Men identity-transfer, weirwood-bonding, dragon-bonding, resurrection, curse-laying) that infobox extraction cannot reach. These types are *prose-derived only* — the wiki-infobox parser does not emit them.
324	
325	| Edge Type | Description | Directionality | Wiki Source |
326	|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|
327	| `WARGS_INTO` | A warg / skinchanger actively occupies the consciousness of an animal or person — e.g., Bran into Summer / Hodor / the heart tree; Arya into Nymeria (dream-skinchange); Varamyr into his wolf / eagle / shadowcat; Orell into his eagle; Jon (low-key) into Ghost | Warg → Vessel | — |
328	| `BONDED_TO` | Static magical bond between two beings — broader and more permanent than `WARGS_INTO` (which is the active occupation moment). Covers dragon-rider bonds (Daenerys ↔ Drogon, etc.), warg-animal lifelong pairing (Bran ↔ Summer when not actively warging), weirwood-bond (Bran ↔ his three-eyed-crow / weirwood network) | Symmetric | — |
329	| `SACRIFICES` | Deliberate ritual or magical killing with supernatural/symbolic purpose — distinct from `KILLS` (combat) and `EXECUTES` (judicial). Mirri Maz Duur sacrificing Drogo's life-essence; Daenerys sacrificing her unborn child to magic the dragon eggs; Stannis (via Melisandre) sacrificing Edric Storm's leech-blood / Mance / Penny's brother / (theory-tier) Shireen; Craster sacrificing his sons to the Others | Sacrificer → Victim | — |
330	| `RESURRECTS` | Returns the dead to life via supernatural means — distinct from `HEALS` (medical), distinct from `KILLED_BY` (semantic reverse). Thoros of Myr resurrects Beric Dondarrion (multiple times); Beric resurrects Catelyn → Lady Stoneheart (ASOS Epilogue); Coldhands resurrected by unknown force (Children?); Patchface drowned-and-returned; Qyburn reanimates the Mountain; Red Priests broadly perform this (Thoros, Moqorro) — `HEALS` is for body-restoration, `RESURRECTS` is for death-reversal | Resurrector → Resurrected | — |
331	| `CURSES` | A character or magical force lays a curse — Mirri Maz Duur's "when the sun rises in the west" curse on Daenerys; Maggy the Frog's Valonqar prophecy-curse on Cersei; the Curse of Harrenhal (collective); Night's King lore | Curser → Cursed | — |
332	| `PRACTICES` | Character actively practices a named magical or ritual discipline. Target is `concept.magic` (or `concept.craft` for non-magical-but-named-discipline cases). Distinct from `WARGS_INTO` (active occupation moment), `BONDED_TO` (static pairing), `WORSHIPS` (religious devotion, not the magical practice itself), `CLERGY_OF` (religious office). Examples: Melisandre/shadow-binding, Mirri Maz Duur/maegi-blood-magic, Bran/greendreams, faceless-men/identity-transfer, Qyburn/necromancy. | Character → Magic discipline | — |

404

406	
407	| Field | Purpose | Example |
408	|-------|---------|---------|
409	| `type` | Edge type from taxonomy above | `KILLS` |

411	| `target` | Target node | `aerys-ii-targaryen` |
412	| `first_available` | Spoiler gate — earliest book/chapter where this is known | `AGOT Jaime I` |
413	| `confidence` | Tier 1-5 | `1` |
414	| `evidence` | Chapter citation or wiki URL | `AGOT Eddard XV` |
415	| `notes` | Qualifiers, context, temporal bounds | `"while serving as Kingsguard"` |
416	| `temporal` | When this edge is active (if not permanent) | `"until ASOS"`, `"during Robert's Rebellion"` |
417	| `symmetric` | Whether the edge is bidirectional | `true` / `false` |

420	
421	## Confidence Tiers
422	
423	Every claim in the system has a confidence tier:
424	
425	| Tier | Label | Description | Treatment |
426	|------|-------|-------------|-----------|
427	| 1 | Verified Canon | Explicitly stated in text, no ambiguity | Ground truth |
428	| 2 | Strong Inference | Not stated directly, but inferable with high confidence | Near-fact, note the inferential step |
429	| 3 | Community Consensus | Widely accepted theory with strong textual support | Leading theory, flag as unconfirmed |
430	| 4 | Plausible Speculation | Reasonable theory with some support, significant uncertainty | Possibility, note competing interpretations |
431	| 5 | Crackpot | Entertaining, minimal evidence | Include, clearly label |
432

434	
435	## Spoiler Gating — DEFERRED to Post-First-Release
436	
437	> **Status (2026-04-27):** Spoiler gating via `first_available` is **deferred** to a post-first-release backfill pass. The field is **optional** in v1 nodes. Existing values may be missing, in inconsistent shapes, or wrong (the wiki-infobox-parser produces systematically wrong values for some page classes — e.g., Tyrion and Varys both got `ADWD` despite appearing from AGOT). **Do not invest context reasoning out individual values during extraction or curation work.** Backfill will happen via a deterministic script after the first release, when the data model is stable enough to enforce consistently.
438	>
439	> When backfill runs, it will use the wiki data sources documented below to derive `first_available` mechanically across the entire node corpus in one pass, rather than relying on per-node agent judgment.
440	
441	### Format (when re-introduced)
442	
443	`{BOOK} {POV} {CHAPTER_NUMBER}` or `{BOOK} Prologue/Epilogue`
444	
445	Examples:
446	- `first_available: AGOT Bran II` (Bran's fall — available from this chapter onward)
447	- `first_available: ASOS Epilogue` (Lady Stoneheart reveal)
448	- `first_available: pre-agot` (D&E novellas and pre-series information)
449	
450	The system will filter content to a user-declared spoiler ceiling. Any node with `first_available` above the ceiling is excluded from retrieval. **This filtering is not active in v1.**
451	
452	**Retroactive significance:** Some information has retroactive importance. A fact available in AGOT may only become meaningful in ADWD. Use `significance_unlocked` as a secondary field:
453	- `first_available: AGOT Eddard I` / `significance_unlocked: ADWD` (Ned's internal guilt about Jon)
454	
455	### Wiki Data Source for Future Backfill
456	
457	The AWOIAF wiki provides two structured sources for the eventual backfill script:
458	
459	**1. Infobox "Books" field** (book-level granularity):
460	```html
461	<li><a href="...">A Game of Thrones</a> <small>(POV)</small></li>
462	<li><a href="...">A Clash of Kings</a> <small>(mentioned)</small></li>
463	```
464	Appearance types: `POV`, `appears`, `mentioned`. First non-"mentioned" book → book-level `first_available`.
465	
466	**2. Citation anchor IDs** (chapter-level granularity):
467	Format: `R{book_abbrev}{chapter_number}` in cite_ref HTML anchors. Example: `cite_ref-Ragot2` → AGOT chapter 2. Lowest-numbered citation per page → chapter-level `first_available`.
468	
469	**Known parser-bug class:** the lowest-cite_ref heuristic produces wrong values for some pages (cite_refs are reordered when wiki footnotes are edited; ADWD-era references can sort first). The backfill script must cross-reference cite_refs against `pass1_mentions` to detect and correct these silent failures, OR simply use `Books` field as the primary signal and treat cite_refs as refinement only.
470	
471	**Coverage at v1:** 5,279 of 17,657 cached wiki pages have infoboxes. The Track B parser populated `first_available` for 2,888 of those (54.7%) — values not yet validated.

475	## Pass 1 Extraction Schema (v2)
476	
477	Each mechanical extraction produces a `.extraction.md` file with these sections:
478	
479	| Section | Purpose |
480	|---------|---------|
481	| Chapter Metadata | Book, chapter number, POV, spoiler gate, locations, timeline, `time_markers` |
482	| Physical Environment | Weather, season, time of day, lighting, sounds, smells, sensory details |
483	| Characters Present | Who appears in the chapter with role and notes |
484	| Character Appearances | Physical descriptions as given in this chapter: hair, eyes, build, scars, clothing, weapons, age |
485	| Characters Referenced | Who is mentioned but not present |
486	| Locations | Location routing table (name, role, first appearance) |
487	| Location Descriptions | Defensive features, architecture, interiors, scale, condition, terrain, sensory details |
488	| Artifacts & Objects | Objects of narrative significance |
489	| Food & Drink | All meals/food described: dishes, ingredients, who eats with whom, preparation |
490	| Hospitality & Guest Right | Guest right invocations, bread and salt, shelter, violations |
491	| Events & Actions | Numbered sequence of chapter events |
492	| Spatial Layout & Movement | Phase-based positioning table (Opening, Advance, Ambush, Assembly, etc.) |
493	| Information Revealed | What the reader/characters learn, with knowledge asymmetry tracking |
494	| Dialogue of Note | Key quotes with speaker, listener, context |
495	| POV Internal State | Emotional state, preoccupations, decisions, self-deception |
496	| Relationships Observed | Character-to-character relationships with evidence |
497	| Unanswered Questions | Open questions raised by the chapter |
498	| Raw Entity List | Flat index across 10 categories: Characters, Locations, Houses, Factions & Organizations, Religions & Faiths, Cultures & Peoples, Artifacts & Objects, In-world Texts & Songs, Magic & Phenomena, Wars & Conflicts, Titles & Offices |
499	
500	Full schema definition and extraction rules: `.claude/agents/mechanical-extractor.md`

503	
504	## Agent Conventions
505	
506	All agents working in this project should:
507	
508	1. **Load this file first** — understand the data model before doing anything
509	2. **Follow the file naming conventions** — consistency is critical
510	3. **Tag every output with confidence tiers** — nothing goes untiered
511	4. **Include `first_available` on every node and edge** — spoiler gating is architectural, not optional
512	5. **Output structured markdown** — follow the schemas defined in each agent prompt file
513	6. **Propose, don't decide** — analytical findings go to the curation queue as candidates, not as accepted facts
514	7. **Cite chapter sources** — every claim traces back to a specific chapter or wiki page
515	8. **Direwolves and dragons are characters** — Ghost, Grey Wind, Lady, Nymeria, Summer, Shaggydog, Drogon, Rhaegal, Viserion are characters with agency and narrative arcs, not creatures or fauna
516	9. **Edge taxonomy is for the graph layer, not Pass 1** — Pass 1 (mechanical extraction) records relationships in free-text natural language. Pass 2+, wiki ingestion, and graph-building use the controlled edge vocabulary from this file. If you're building graph edges and no type fits, flag it for taxonomy expansion rather than inventing ad-hoc labels
517

519	
520	## Artifact Formats by Consumer
521	
522	> **Principle:** Markdown for things that become node content. Structured (JSONL/JSON) for things consumed by code. Don't force one format across all artifact types.
523	>
524	> Wiki Pass 2 produces a per-page family of intermediate artifacts at `working/wiki/pass2-buckets/<bucket_id>/<artifact-dir>/<slug>.<ext>`. Each artifact has exactly one writer (single-writer-per-file invariant). The launcher's promotion step is the only process that combines them into the final node.
525	
526	| Artifact | Format | Producer | Format choice why |
527	|----------|--------|----------|-------------------|
528	| `skeleton/<slug>.node.md` | markdown | Python emitter (`scripts/wiki-pass2-emit-deterministic.py`) | becomes the head of the final node — frontmatter + thin Identity + full Edges from infobox |
529	| `prose/<slug>.prose.md` | markdown | Python extractor (`scripts/wiki-pass2-extract-prose.py`) | concatenated onto skeleton at promotion — narrative body sections from wiki HTML |
530	| `prose-edges/<slug>.edges.jsonl` | JSONL | Stage 4 agent (future) | structured rows `{source, edge_type, target, qualifier, citation}` consumed by graph build, not by readers |
531	| `entity-index/<slug>.index.json` | JSON | post-promotion script (future) | trigger-table input — alias/title/name → slug lookup for Pass 3+ extractions |
532	
533	**Promotion rule:** the launcher concatenates `skeleton/<slug>.node.md + "\n" + prose/<slug>.prose.md` (when prose exists) and atomic-renames into `graph/nodes/<type>/<slug>.node.md`. Tier-B pages with no prose file get the skeleton verbatim. Stage 4 + entity-index artifacts attach later via separate promotion steps.
534	
535	**Investigation tooling:** `scripts/graph-query.py <slug>` — read-only CLI that prints a node's frontmatter, outbound edges (with target-resolution status: OK / ALIAS→ / ORPHAN), and top inbound references from `working/wiki/data/cross-references.jsonl`. Use this before grepping raw markdown when investigating any single node. Modes: default full, `--edges-only`, `--inbound-only`, `--json`.
536	
537	**Why single-writer-per-file matters:** if two processes (e.g., Python skeleton emitter + LLM prose-fill agent) both wrote to the same file, the LLM's tendency to paraphrase or normalize would corrupt the deterministic prefix. Separating artifacts by writer makes that class of failure structurally impossible. A validator never has to enforce byte-equality; the concatenation is the only path that produces the final node.
538	
539	---
540	
541	## Wiki Infobox Fields → Edge Type Mapping
542	
543	> **Vocabulary lock — read this before adding or renaming any edge type.**
544	>
545	> **There are two related vocabularies in this document, and it matters which one you mean.**
546	>
547	> 1. **Master edge vocabulary** — the union of all subsections under `## Edge Types (Relationship Categories)` above. Currently **~163 distinct edge types** across 15 categories (kinship, political, factional, military, knowledge, emotional/perceptual, spatial, possession, identity, cultural, narrative, prophecy, evidentiary, causal, hospitality, magic-and-supernatural). For an authoritative live count, run `scripts/build-edge-type-counts.py` — its `canonical_type_count` is derived from this file. Session 54 (2026-05-15) added `UNCLE_OF`, `NEPHEW_OF`, `KILLED_WITH`, `ATTENDS` after Stage 4 batch-0012 vocab-gap audit. Session 55 first wave (2026-05-16) added `COUSIN_OF`, `MILK_BROTHER_OF`, `NURSED_BY`, `WET_NURSE_OF`, `KNIGHTED_BY`, `BESTOWS_KNIGHTHOOD_ON`, `DEPICTED_IN` after Stage 4 batches 0012-0018 surfaced these recurring patterns. **Session 55 second wave (2026-05-18) — vocab FINAL**: added 17 types (`AFFLICTED_BY`, `DIED_OF`, `COMPANION_OF`, `PARTICIPATES_IN`, `OFFICIATES`, `ATTACKS`, `ASSAULTS`, `COURTS`, `CONTRACTED_WITH`, `PROPOSED_AS_BRIDE`, `CROWNS_QUEEN_OF_LOVE_AND_BEAUTY`, `PRACTICES`, `PURCHASED_FROM`, `BUILT`, `CAPTAIN_OF`, `CREW_OF`, `REPUTED_AS`) + 2 description mods (`FIGHTS_IN` extended to "battle, war, or tournament as a combatant"; `MANIPULATES` qualifier-mechanism note). After this wave the classifier prompt flips its gap-filing default to FINAL — vocab-gap questions are no longer filed for remaining batches; non-fitting candidates reject as `no-fitting-type-vocab-locked`. **Session 58 — vocab completeness audit (2026-05-19)**: added 10 types (`SPIES_ON`, `INFORMS`, `NAMED_AFTER`, `STEP_PARENT_OF`, `STEP_CHILD_OF`, `IN_LAW_OF`, `RESCUES`, `BANISHES`, `TORTURES`, `CONSPIRES_WITH`) from vocab-completeness audit of 7,398 P1 rows + 4,207 Stage-4 emits + 135 `no-fitting-type` rejections; vocab 149 → 159. **Session 61 — Stage 4 Haiku residual-resolve patterns (2026-05-19)**: added 5 types (`IMPRISONED_AT`, `TRAVELS_WITH`, `PRISONER_EXCHANGE_FOR`, `GUARDS`, `ENCOUNTERS`) from Stage 4 Haiku residual-resolve surfacing recurring patterns that didn't fit existing vocab; vocab 159 → 164. `ENCOUNTERS` introduces the first **validator-enforced verb gate** (classify-prompt Rule 6) — promotes the prompt-text behavioral constraint pattern (precedent: Rule 2 KNOWS-STOP) to schema enforcement. Normalizer alias `ACCOMPANIES` → `TRAVELS_WITH` added the same session. **Session 63 — KNOWS deprecation (2026-05-21)**: removed `KNOWS` from the active vocabulary (vocab 164 → 163) after Stage 4 overnight run showed 82.3% fallback rate; deferred to a future Pass-1-derived chapter co-occurrence pass. Also added partial-coverage scope-note to `ENCOUNTERS` (wiki-prose register captures only staged meetings, not all real ones). Some types (`FEARS`, `MOURNS`, `IMPERSONATES`, `FORESHADOWS`, `DREAMS_OF`, `WARGS_INTO`, `RESURRECTS`, `MADE_OF`, `LOOTED_BY`, `GIFTED_TO`, etc.) are pre-declared for prose-derived passes and currently have zero instances in the graph — that's expected; they're reserved for Stage 4 classification. This vocabulary is the **single source of truth for every emitter** — Python parsers, Pass-1 mechanical extractor, prose-edge-classifier, voice-analyzer, foreshadowing-scanner, every script, every agent.
548	> 2. **Wiki infobox subset** — the table below, mapping wiki infobox FIELD names to edge types. Currently **26 distinct edge types**, all of which are also in the master vocabulary. The parser at `scripts/wiki-infobox-parser.py` (`FIELD_EDGE_MAP` dict) implements only this subset, because infobox fields are the only signal it sees. Prose-derived edges are NOT restricted to this subset — they may emit any of the master vocabulary types (including the new Magic & Supernatural subsection added Session 53).
549	>
550	> **Why locked:** the graph's value comes from being able to traverse `SPOUSE_OF` everywhere consistently. If one source emits `SPOUSE_OF` and another emits `MARRIED_TO`, traversal breaks. The master edge types were chosen deliberately (curated from infobox-field frequencies + narrative/perception/prophecy needs for later passes, with Magic & Supernatural added Session 53 ahead of Stage 4); expanding the set requires the same deliberation — propose via `curation/edge-vocabulary-candidates.md`, get approval, then update this file + parser + classifier prompt.
551	>
552	> **No emitter invents edge types.** Scripts and agents emit ONLY from the master vocabulary. As of the Session 55 second wave (2026-05-18) the vocabulary is **FINAL** for the Stage 4 bulk run — agents that encounter a non-fitting relationship reject it as `no-fitting-type-vocab-locked` rather than filing new `vocabulary-gap` questions. The gap-filing channel (`working/wiki/pass2-buckets/questions-for-matt.jsonl`) is closed for Stage 4 v1; reopen only if a subsequent corpus expansion (cross-book Pass 1 retro, Pass 3 voice/perception passes, etc.) surfaces a recurring pattern that genuinely cannot be expressed in any of the ~159 canonical types.
553	>
554	> **Adding a new edge type:** append a row to the appropriate `## Edge Types` subsection FIRST. If the new type comes from a wiki infobox field, also add the field → edge_type mapping to `FIELD_EDGE_MAP` in `scripts/wiki-infobox-parser.py` and add a row to the wiki-infobox subset table below. Then re-run the affected emitter. Don't shortcut the order.
555	>
556	> **Reverse-direction emissions** (e.g., `HELD_BY` on a title node pointing back to people who held it, `KILLED_BY` on a victim pointing to killer, `DECEIVED_BY` on a target pointing to deceiver) are permitted and equivalent to the forward edge with directionality swapped. They are not separate types — query layers should treat `HELD_BY(a→b)` as identical to `HOLDS_TITLE(b→a)`. Reverse pairs are documented in their respective subsection notes.
557	>
558	> **Currently unmapped infobox fields** (deliberately deferred — see `working/todos.md` "Edge taxonomy gaps"): `dynasty` (222 pages), `written by` (168), `hatched` (8), `fathers` plural (21), `vassal` (8), `cadet branch` singular (11). These need taxonomy decisions before mapping.
559	>
560	> **Edge polish phase (future):** semantically-equivalent variants that crept in via different infobox fields (e.g., `Predecessor`/`Successor` both producing `SUCCEEDS` in different directions, or any future near-duplicates) get reviewed and merged by an agent reasoning step. That review happens AFTER all wiki ingestion completes — not during. Stage 3a / Stage 3b never merge edges.
561	
562	When Pass 2 (wiki ingestion) processes wiki pages, these infobox fields map to edge types:
563	
564	| Wiki Infobox Field | Edge Type | Notes |
565	|-------------------|-----------|-------|
566	| Father, Mother | `PARENT_OF` | Reverse direction (wiki lists parent on child's page) |
567	| Spouse, Spouses | `SPOUSE_OF` | |
568	| Lover, Lovers | `LOVER_OF` | |
569	| Issue | `PARENT_OF` | Forward direction (wiki lists children on parent's page) |
570	| Allegiance, Allegiances | `SWORN_TO` | |
571	| Overlord, Overlords | `OVERLORD_OF` | Reverse direction |
572	| Culture | `CULTURE_OF` | |
573	| Religion | `WORSHIPS` | For characters; `RELIGION_OF` for locations |
574	| Seat, Seats | `SEAT_OF` | |
575	| Head | `RULES` | House head rules the house |
576	| Heir, Heirs | `HEIR_TO` | |
577	| Founder | `FOUNDED` | |
578	| Successor | `SUCCEEDS` | |
579	| Predecessor | `SUCCEEDS` | Reverse direction |
580	| Ancestral weapon | `ANCESTRAL_WEAPON_OF` | |
581	| Cadet branches | `CADET_BRANCH_OF` | |
582	| Owner, Owners | `OWNS` | For animals, ships, etc. |
583	| Monarch | `SWORN_TO` or `SERVES` | Context-dependent |
584	| Born | `BORN_AT` | If location extractable |
585	| Died | `DIED_AT` | If location extractable |
586	| Buried | `BURIED_AT` | |
587	| Alias, Aliases | `ALIAS_OF` | |
588	| Ruler | `RULES` | |
589	| Region, Regions | `REGION_OF` | |
590	| Species | Node type metadata | Not an edge — informs entity type |
591	| Conflict, Battles | `FIGHTS_IN` | For battle pages and characters' battle lists |
592	| Conflict (on a war page's battle list) | `PART_OF` | Reverse direction — battle → war containment; emit when parsing a war page's list of constituent battles |
593	| Result | `DEFEATS` | Extract victor/defeated from result text |
594	| Written by | `WRITTEN_BY` | For in-world texts (books, songs, decrees); subject = text, target = author |

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""
3	stage4-classify-manderly.py
4	
5	Classifies enriched prose-edge candidates for the characters-house-manderly bucket
6	into emit_edge or reject_just_mention decisions.
7	
8	Input files (from prose-edge-candidates-enriched/):
9	  - theomore-manderly.candidates.jsonl
10	  - therry.candidates.jsonl
11	  - warrick-manderly.candidates.jsonl
12	  - wendel-manderly.candidates.jsonl
13	  - wylis-manderly.candidates.jsonl
14	
15	Output files (to prose-edges-haiku/):
16	  - <slug>.edges.jsonl
17	
18	Decision logic:
19	  - emit_edge: evidence explicitly states a typed relationship between source and target
20	  - reject_just_mention: target mentioned for context only, or relationship cannot be
21	    expressed in the locked vocabulary
22	
23	Key rules enforced:
24	  - valid_edge_types per row is the permissible set; only those types may be emitted
25	  - ENCOUNTERS requires staging_verbs_present to be non-empty (verb gate)
26	  - KNOWS is deprecated; apparent KNOWS -> knows-deprecated-defer-to-pass1
27	  - Tier-1 edges (SIBLING_OF, SPOUSE_OF, PARENT_OF, WARD_OF, HOLDS_TITLE, VOWS_TO,
28	    MANIPULATES, SWORN_TO) REQUIRE a qualifier field
29	  - Tier-3 edges MUST NOT have a qualifier field
30	  - confidence_tier is always 1 for wiki prose
31	  - evidence_kind is always "wiki-entity"
32	  - evidence_snippet is verbatim from evidence_paragraph, <= 200 chars
33	  - The source character is the owner of the wiki page being classified;
34	    evidence_paragraph describes events in that character's biography.
35	    Relationship checkers MUST verify the relationship is between source and target,
36	    not between two third parties mentioned in the same paragraph.
37	"""
38	
39	import json
40	import re
41	import argparse
42	import sys
43	from pathlib import Path
44	
45	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
46	# Constants
47	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
48	
49	BUCKET = "characters-house-manderly"
50	BUCKET_DIR = Path(
51	    "/Users/mnoth/source/asoiaf-chat/working/wiki/pass2-buckets"
52	) / BUCKET
53	
54	INPUT_DIR = BUCKET_DIR / "prose-edge-candidates-enriched"
55	OUTPUT_DIR = BUCKET_DIR / "prose-edges-haiku"
56	
57	TARGET_FILES = [
58	    "theomore-manderly.candidates.jsonl",
59	    "therry.candidates.jsonl",
60	    "warrick-manderly.candidates.jsonl",
61	    "wendel-manderly.candidates.jsonl",
62	    "wylis-manderly.candidates.jsonl",
63	]
64	
65	# Tier-1 edges: qualifier REQUIRED
66	TIER1_EDGES = frozenset({
67	    "SIBLING_OF",
68	    "SPOUSE_OF",
69	    "PARENT_OF",
70	    "WARD_OF",
71	    "HOLDS_TITLE",
72	    "VOWS_TO",
73	    "MANIPULATES",
74	    "SWORN_TO",
75	})
76	
77	CHARACTER_TYPES = frozenset({
78	    "character.human",
79	    "character.direwolf",
80	    "character.dragon",
81	})
82	
83	EVENT_TYPES = frozenset({
84	    "event.battle",
85	    "event.war",
86	    "event.tournament",
87	})
88	
89	LOCATION_TYPES = frozenset({
90	    "place.location",
91	    "place.region",
92	})
93	
94	ORG_TYPES = frozenset({
95	    "organization.house",
96	    "organization.faction",
97	    "organization.religion",
98	})
99	
100	TITLE_TYPE = "title"
101	CONCEPT_CUSTOM_TYPE = "concept.custom"
102	SPECIES_TYPE = "species"
103	MATERIAL_TYPE = "object.material"
104	FOOD_TYPE = "object.food"
105	CONCEPT_MAGIC_TYPE = "concept.magic"
106	MEDICAL_TYPE = "concept.medical"
107	CONCEPT_TYPE = "concept.culture"
108	
109	
110	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
111	# Output helpers
112	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
113	
114	def make_snippet(paragraph: str) -> str:
115	    """Return the first 200 characters of the evidence paragraph."""
116	    return paragraph[:200]
117	
118	
119	def reject(source_slug: str, target_slug: str, reason: str) -> dict:
120	    return {
121	        "decision": "reject_just_mention",
122	        "candidate_kind": "source_target",
123	        "source_slug": source_slug,
124	        "target_slug": target_slug,
125	        "reason": reason,
126	    }
127	
128	
129	def emit(
130	    source_slug: str,
131	    target_slug: str,
132	    edge_type: str,
133	    evidence_paragraph: str,
134	    source_section: str,
135	    qualifier: str | None = None,
136	) -> dict:
137	    row = {
138	        "decision": "emit_edge",
139	        "candidate_kind": "source_target",
140	        "evidence_kind": "wiki-entity",
141	        "source_slug": source_slug,
142	        "target_slug": target_slug,
143	        "edge_type": edge_type,
144	        "evidence_snippet": make_snippet(evidence_paragraph),
145	        "evidence_section": source_section,
146	        "confidence_tier": 1,
147	    }
148	    if qualifier is not None:
149	        row["qualifier"] = qualifier
150	    return row
151	
152	
153	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
154	# Name extraction helpers
155	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
156	
157	def source_first_name(source_slug: str) -> str:
158	    """e.g. 'wylis-manderly' -> 'wylis'"""
159	    return source_slug.split("-")[0].lower()
160	
161	
162	def target_first_name(target_slug: str) -> str:
163	    """e.g. 'tywin-lannister' -> 'tywin'"""
164	    return target_slug.split("-")[0].lower()
165	
166	
167	def target_display_name(target_slug: str) -> str:
168	    """e.g. 'house-frey' -> 'frey', 'robb-stark' -> 'robb stark'"""
169	    parts = target_slug.split("-")
170	    # Strip leading "house-" etc.
171	    if parts[0] in ("house", "ser", "lord"):
172	        parts = parts[1:]
173	    return " ".join(parts).lower()
174	
175	
176	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
177	# Core classification dispatcher
178	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
179	
180	def classify(row: dict) -> dict:
181	    """Classify a single candidate row. Returns a decision dict."""
182	    source = row["source_slug"]
183	    target = row["target_slug"]
184	    evidence = row.get("evidence_paragraph", "")
185	    target_type = row.get("target_type", "")
186	    valid_types = set(row.get("valid_edge_types", []))
187	    staging_verbs = row.get("staging_verbs_present", [])
188	    section = row.get("source_section", "")
189	    ev = evidence.lower()
190	
191	    sfn = source_first_name(source)  # e.g. "wylis"
192	    tfn = target_first_name(target)  # e.g. "tywin"
193	
194	    def can(etype: str) -> bool:
195	        return etype in valid_types
196	
197	    def e(edge_type: str, qualifier: str | None = None) -> dict:
198	        return emit(source, target, edge_type, evidence, section, qualifier)
199	
200	    def r(reason: str) -> dict:
201	        return reject(source, target, reason)
202	
203	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
204	    # DEPRECATED: KNOWS
205	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
206	    # No KNOWS edge type exists. If something would have been KNOWS, reject.
207	
208	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
209	    # REJECT BY TARGET TYPE: settings / objects / materials / food / custom
210	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
211	    if target_type in (MATERIAL_TYPE, FOOD_TYPE, SPECIES_TYPE):
212	        return r("material/food/species mentioned in narrative setting")
213	
214	    if target_type == CONCEPT_CUSTOM_TYPE:
215	        # fosterage concept: check if source was fostered / fostered someone
216	        if target == "fosterage" and can("WARD_OF"):
217	            if f"fostered" in ev and sfn in ev:
218	                return r("fosterage custom mentioned in context of others, not source directly")
219	        return r("custom/concept mentioned in narrative setting")
220	
221	    if target_type in ("concept.culture", "concept.language"):
222	        return r("culture/language concept mentioned in narrative setting")
223	
224	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
225	    # LOCATIONS
226	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
227	    if target_type in LOCATION_TYPES:
228	        loc = target_display_name(target)
229	        # BORN_AT
230	        if can("BORN_AT"):
231	            if any(p in ev for p in [f"{sfn} was born", f"{sfn} born at", f"{sfn} born in"]):
232	                if tfn in ev or loc in ev:
233	                    return e("BORN_AT")
234	        # DIED_AT
235	        if can("DIED_AT"):
236	            if any(p in ev for p in [f"{sfn} died at", f"{sfn} died in", f"{sfn}'s death at"]):
237	                if tfn in ev or loc in ev:
238	                    return e("DIED_AT")
239	        # IMPRISONED_AT
240	        if can("IMPRISONED_AT"):
241	            if (f"{sfn}" in ev and loc in ev and
242	                    any(p in ev for p in [
243	                        "held captive at", "held captive in", "prisoner at",
244	                        "imprisoned at", "captive at", "captivity in"
245	                    ])):
246	                return e("IMPRISONED_AT")
247	        # BURIED_AT
248	        if can("BURIED_AT"):
249	            if f"{sfn}" in ev and loc in ev and any(p in ev for p in [
250	                "interred in", "buried at", "buried in", "remains are interred",
251	                "bones are interred", "bones are returned",
252	                f"{sfn}'s bones", f"{sfn}'s remains",
253	            ]):
254	                return e("BURIED_AT")
255	        # TRAVELS_TO — source explicitly traveled to or sailed to/from this location
256	        if can("TRAVELS_TO"):
257	            if f"{sfn}" in ev and loc in ev and any(p in ev for p in [
258	                f"{sfn} sailed to", f"{sfn} sailed from", f"{sfn} traveled to",
259	                f"{sfn} travelled to", f"{sfn} travels to", f"{sfn} rode to",
260	                f"{sfn} journeyed to", f"{sfn} went to",
261	                # "sailed from White Harbor" where source is the implied traveler
262	                f"sailed from {loc}", f"sailed to {loc}",
263	            ]):
264	                return e("TRAVELS_TO")
265	        return r("location mentioned in narrative setting")
266	
267	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
268	    # MEDICAL
269	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
270	    if target_type == MEDICAL_TYPE:
271	        if can("DIED_OF"):
272	            if f"{sfn}" in ev and any(p in ev for p in ["died of", "death from", "died from"]):
273	                return e("DIED_OF")
274	        if can("AFFLICTED_BY"):
275	            if f"{sfn}" in ev and any(p in ev for p in ["suffered", "afflicted", "stricken"]):
276	                return e("AFFLICTED_BY")
277	        return r("medical concept mentioned without AFFLICTED_BY/DIED_OF signal")
278	
279	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
280	    # CONCEPT.MAGIC
281	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
282	    if target_type == CONCEPT_MAGIC_TYPE:
283	        return r("magic concept mentioned in narrative context")
284	
285	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
286	    # TITLES
287	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
288	    if target_type == TITLE_TYPE:
289	        if can("HOLDS_TITLE"):
290	            # Source must be directly described as holding this title
291	            if _source_holds_title(ev, sfn, target):
292	                return e("HOLDS_TITLE", qualifier="current")
293	        return r("title mentioned in narrative context; source does not hold it directly")
294	
295	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
296	    # CHARACTERS
297	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
298	    if target_type in CHARACTER_TYPES:
299	        return _classify_character_target(row, source, target, sfn, tfn, ev, valid_types, can, e, r, staging_verbs, section, evidence)
300	
301	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
302	    # EVENTS
303	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
304	    if target_type in EVENT_TYPES:
305	        return _classify_event_target(row, source, target, sfn, tfn, ev, valid_types, can, e, r)
306	
307	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
308	    # ORGANIZATIONS (houses, factions, religions)
309	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
310	    if target_type in ORG_TYPES:
311	        return _classify_org_target(row, source, target, sfn, tfn, ev, valid_types, can, e, r)
312	
313	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
314	    # Fallback
315	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
316	    return r("no-fitting-type-vocab-locked")
317	
318	
319	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
320	# HOLDS_TITLE checker
321	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
322	
323	def _source_holds_title(ev: str, sfn: str, target_slug: str) -> bool:
324	    """
325	    Return True ONLY if the evidence explicitly describes the SOURCE as holding
326	    the named title.
327	
328	    Strict rules:
329	    - Source must be the grammatical subject of the title-attribution.
330	    - Do NOT fire if title is attributed to third parties (e.g., "his household knights",
331	      "Robb is King in the North", "Lord Walder Frey").
332	    - Do NOT fire for compound titles held by others (e.g., Theomore has household knights,
333	      not IS a household knight).
334	    """
335	    target_lower = target_slug.lower().replace("-", " ")
336	
337	    # Reject possessive patterns like "source's [title]" — source has subordinates
338	    # with that title, doesn't hold it themselves
339	    if f"{sfn}'s {target_lower}" in ev or f"lord {sfn}'s {target_lower}" in ev:
340	        return False
341	
342	    # Pattern A: "[source] is/was a/the [title]" — direct attribution
343	    for p in [
344	        f"{sfn} is a {target_lower}", f"{sfn} was a {target_lower}",
345	        f"{sfn} is the {target_lower}", f"{sfn} was the {target_lower}",
346	        f"{sfn} is an {target_lower}", f"{sfn} was an {target_lower}",
347	    ]:
348	        if p in ev:
349	            return True
350	
351	    # Pattern B: "The [title] is/was/wields/acts..." in a sentence where ONLY source is named
352	    # (i.e., "the knight" is a referring expression for source)
353	    referring_patterns = [
354	        f"the {target_lower} is", f"the {target_lower} was",
355	        f"the {target_lower} wields", f"the {target_lower} leads",
356	        f"the {target_lower} acts", f"the {target_lower} rides",
357	        f"the unwed {target_lower}", f"the skilled {target_lower}",
358	        f"a canny {target_lower}", f"a skilled {target_lower}",
359	    ]
360	    for p in referring_patterns:
361	        if p not in ev:
362	            continue
363	        sentences = [s.strip() for s in ev.split(".") if s.strip()]
364	        for sent in sentences:
365	            if p not in sent:
366	                continue
367	            # Source must appear in the same sentence
368	            if sfn not in sent:
369	                continue
370	            # Reject if the title is in a possessive of someone else:
371	            # e.g. "[other]'s knight" or "his household knights"
372	            if f"'s {target_lower}" in sent or f"his {target_lower}" in sent or f"her {target_lower}" in sent:
373	                continue
374	            return True
375	
376	    # Pattern C: "ser [source]" or "lord [source]" (only for generic titles knight/lord)
377	    if target_slug == "knight" and f"ser {sfn}" in ev:
378	        return True
379	    if target_slug == "lord" and f"lord {sfn}" in ev:
380	        return True
381	
382	    return False
383	
384	
385	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
386	# Character target classifier
387	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
388	
389	def _classify_character_target(
390	    row, source, target, sfn, tfn, ev, valid_types, can, e, r, staging_verbs, section, evidence
391	) -> dict:
392	    """Classify a candidate where target_type is character.*"""
393	
394	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
395	    # SIBLING_OF - require explicit sibling phrase with BOTH names or
396	    # clear "older/younger brother/sister, Ser/Lord [target_name]" structure
397	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
398	    if can("SIBLING_OF"):
399	        qual = _check_sibling_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn)
400	        if qual:
401	            return e("SIBLING_OF", qualifier=qual)
402	
403	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
404	    # PARENT_OF - source is parent of target
405	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
406	    if can("PARENT_OF"):
407	        qual = _check_parent_of_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn)
408	        if qual:
409	            return e("PARENT_OF", qualifier=qual)
410	
411	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
412	    # SPOUSE_OF - source is married to target
413	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
414	    if can("SPOUSE_OF"):
415	        qual = _check_spouse_of_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn)
416	        if qual:
417	            return e("SPOUSE_OF", qualifier=qual)
418	
419	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
420	    # BETROTHED_TO - source is betrothed to target
421	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
422	    if can("BETROTHED_TO"):
423	        if _check_betrothed_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
424	            return e("BETROTHED_TO")
425	
426	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
427	    # PRISONER_OF - source is prisoner/captive of target
428	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
429	    if can("PRISONER_OF"):
430	        if _check_prisoner_of_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
431	            return e("PRISONER_OF")
432	
433	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
434	    # PRISONER_EXCHANGE_FOR - source and target are exchanged as prisoners
435	    # Both must be named as the items being exchanged
436	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
437	    if can("PRISONER_EXCHANGE_FOR"):
438	        if _check_prisoner_exchange_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
439	            return e("PRISONER_EXCHANGE_FOR")
440	
441	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
442	    # CAPTURES - source captures target in combat/battle
443	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
444	    if can("CAPTURES"):
445	        if _check_captures_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
446	            return e("CAPTURES")
447	
448	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
449	    # KILLS / KILLED_BY
450	    # Require: explicit "source kills/killed/slew target" pattern
451	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
452	    if can("KILLS"):
453	        if _check_kills_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
454	            return e("KILLS")
455	
456	    # KILLED_BY is inverse; source was killed by target
457	    if can("KILLED_BY"):
458	        if _check_killed_by_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
459	            return e("KILLED_BY")
460	
461	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
462	    # SWORN_TO (character level — sworn to a person)
463	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
464	    if can("SWORN_TO"):
465	        if _check_sworn_to_person_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
466	            return e("SWORN_TO", qualifier="current")
467	
468	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
469	    # SERVES - source serves target
470	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
471	    if can("SERVES"):
472	        if _check_serves_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
473	            return e("SERVES")
474	
475	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
476	    # COMMANDS - source commands target (person)
477	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
478	    if can("COMMANDS"):
479	        if _check_commands_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
480	            return e("COMMANDS")
481	
482	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
483	    # ADVISES - source advises target
484	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
485	    if can("ADVISES"):
486	        if _check_advises_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
487	            return e("ADVISES")
488	
489	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
490	    # BETRAYS - source betrays target
491	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
492	    if can("BETRAYS"):
493	        if _check_betrays_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
494	            return e("BETRAYS")
495	
496	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
497	    # MOURNS - source grieves for/over target
498	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
499	    if can("MOURNS"):
500	        if _check_mourns_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
501	            return e("MOURNS")
502	
503	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
504	    # LOVES - source loves target
505	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
506	    if can("LOVES"):
507	        if _check_loves_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
508	            return e("LOVES")
509	
510	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
511	    # RESPECTS / TRUSTS / DISTRUSTS / FEARS / HATES
512	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
513	    if can("RESPECTS"):
514	        if f"{sfn} respect" in ev and tfn in ev:
515	            return e("RESPECTS")
516	    if can("TRUSTS"):
517	        if f"{sfn} trust" in ev and tfn in ev:
518	            return e("TRUSTS")
519	    if can("DISTRUSTS"):
520	        if f"{sfn} distrust" in ev and tfn in ev:
521	            return e("DISTRUSTS")
522	    if can("FEARS"):
523	        if f"{sfn} fear" in ev and tfn in ev:
524	            return e("FEARS")
525	    if can("HATES"):
526	        if f"{sfn} hates {tfn}" in ev or f"{sfn} hated {tfn}" in ev:
527	            return e("HATES")
528	
529	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
530	    # OPPOSES
531	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
532	    if can("OPPOSES"):
533	        if _check_opposes_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
534	            return e("OPPOSES")
535	
536	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
537	    # DEFEATS
538	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
539	    if can("DEFEATS"):
540	        if _check_defeats_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
541	            return e("DEFEATS")
542	
543	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
544	    # NEGOTIATES_WITH - source directly negotiates with target
545	    # (NOT: third party negotiates with someone that is not the source)
546	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
547	    if can("NEGOTIATES_WITH"):
548	        if _check_negotiates_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
549	            return e("NEGOTIATES_WITH")
550	
551	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
552	    # CONSPIRES_WITH
553	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
554	    if can("CONSPIRES_WITH"):
555	        if f"conspire" in ev and sfn in ev and tfn in ev:
556	            return e("CONSPIRES_WITH")
557	
558	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
559	    # ALLIES_WITH
560	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
561	    if can("ALLIES_WITH"):
562	        if _check_allies_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
563	            return e("ALLIES_WITH")
564	
565	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
566	    # UNCLE_OF / NEPHEW_OF / COUSIN_OF
567	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
568	    if can("UNCLE_OF"):
569	        if _check_uncle_of_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
570	            return e("UNCLE_OF")
571	    if can("NEPHEW_OF"):
572	        if _check_nephew_of_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
573	            return e("NEPHEW_OF")
574	    if can("COUSIN_OF"):
575	        if f"{sfn}'s cousin" in ev and tfn in ev:
576	            return e("COUSIN_OF")
577	        if f"{tfn}'s cousin" in ev and sfn in ev:
578	            return e("COUSIN_OF")
579	
580	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
581	    # WARD_OF - source is ward/foster-child of target
582	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
583	    if can("WARD_OF"):
584	        if _check_ward_of_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
585	            return e("WARD_OF", qualifier="formal")
586	
587	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
588	    # MARRIES_OFF - source arranges marriage for target (or source is married off)
589	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
590	    if can("MARRIES_OFF"):
591	        if _check_marries_off_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
592	            return e("MARRIES_OFF")
593	
594	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
595	    # COURTS
596	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
597	    if can("COURTS"):
598	        if f"{sfn}" in ev and f"{tfn}" in ev and "suitor" in ev:
599	            return e("COURTS")
600	        if f"{sfn} courts {tfn}" in ev:
601	            return e("COURTS")
602	
603	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
604	    # DECEIVES / MANIPULATES
605	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
606	    if can("DECEIVES"):
607	        if f"{sfn} deceiv" in ev and tfn in ev:
608	            return e("DECEIVES")
609	    if can("MANIPULATES"):
610	        if f"{sfn} manipulat" in ev and tfn in ev:
611	            return e("MANIPULATES", qualifier="unknown")
612	
613	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
614	    # RESCUES / HEALS / PROTECTS
615	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
616	    if can("RESCUES"):
617	        if _check_rescues_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
618	            return e("RESCUES")
619	    if can("HEALS"):
620	        if f"{sfn} heal" in ev and tfn in ev:
621	            return e("HEALS")
622	    if can("PROTECTS"):
623	        if f"{sfn} protect" in ev and tfn in ev:
624	            return e("PROTECTS")
625	
626	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
627	    # REVEALS_TO / CONFIDES
628	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
629	    if can("REVEALS_TO"):
630	        if _check_reveals_to_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
631	            return e("REVEALS_TO")
632	
633	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
634	    # ATTENDS - source attends where target is host or target is another attendee
635	    # Only emit if it's an ATTENDS relationship (source→event, not source→person)
636	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
637	    # (for character→character, ATTENDS doesn't make sense; skip)
638	
639	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
640	    # TRAVELS_WITH - source travels in same company as target
641	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
642	    if can("TRAVELS_WITH"):
643	        if _check_travels_with_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
644	            return e("TRAVELS_WITH")
645	
646	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
647	    # COMPANION_OF
648	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
649	    if can("COMPANION_OF"):
650	        if _check_companion_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
651	            return e("COMPANION_OF")
652	
653	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
654	    # ENCOUNTERS - verb gate: staging_verbs_present must be non-empty
655	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
656	    if can("ENCOUNTERS") and staging_verbs:
657	        if _check_encounters_explicit(ev, sfn, tfn):
658	            return e("ENCOUNTERS")
659	
660	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
661	    # No fitting edge found
662	    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
663	    return r("character co-present or mentioned in context without clear typed relationship")
664

666	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
667	# Event target classifier
668	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
669	
670	def _classify_event_target(
671	    row, source, target, sfn, tfn, ev, valid_types, can, e, r
672	) -> dict:
673	    # Build a list of candidate event name variants to match against prose.
674	    # 1. slug with hyphens replaced by spaces: "sistermens-rebellion" -> "sistermens rebellion"
675	    # 2. Same without trailing "s" where apostrophe was dropped: add "sistermen's rebellion"
676	    # 3. The raw slug: "sistermens-rebellion" (exact slug lookup, rarely matches prose)
677	    event_slug_spaced = target.replace("-", " ").lower()
678	    # Build the set of variants the checkers will try
679	    event_names = {event_slug_spaced}
680	    # Add possessive-restored form: replace terminal "s " or "s " preceded by consonant
681	    # Simple heuristic: if a word ends with "s" and the slug dropped an apostrophe,
682	    # the prose form would be "word's".  We try both.
683	    words = event_slug_spaced.split()
684	    possessive_variants = []
685	    for i, word in enumerate(words):
686	        if word.endswith("s") and len(word) > 2:
687	            new_words = words[:]
688	            new_words[i] = word[:-1] + "'s"
689	            possessive_variants.append(" ".join(new_words))
690	    event_names.update(possessive_variants)
691	
692	    def check_event(checker, *args):
693	        for en in event_names:
694	            if checker(ev, sfn, en, *args):
695	                return True
696	        return False
697	
698	    # FIGHTS_IN: source fought in this event
699	    if can("FIGHTS_IN"):
700	        if check_event(_check_fights_in_explicit):
701	            return e("FIGHTS_IN")
702	
703	    # COMMANDS_IN: source held command role in this event
704	    if can("COMMANDS_IN"):
705	        if check_event(_check_commands_in_explicit):
706	            return e("COMMANDS_IN")
707	
708	    # PARTICIPATES_IN: source had non-combat involvement (organizer, escort, etc.)
709	    if can("PARTICIPATES_IN"):
710	        if check_event(_check_participates_in_explicit):
711	            return e("PARTICIPATES_IN")
712	
713	    # ATTENDS: source was present as guest/witness
714	    if can("ATTENDS"):
715	        if check_event(_check_attends_explicit):
716	            return e("ATTENDS")
717	
718	    # OFFICIATES: source performs ritual/ceremonial role
719	    if can("OFFICIATES"):
720	        for en in event_names:
721	            if f"{sfn} officiat" in ev and en in ev:
722	                return e("OFFICIATES")
723	
724	    return r("event mentioned in narrative context without source participation signal")
725	
726	
727	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
728	# Organization target classifier
729	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
730	
731	def _classify_org_target(
732	    row, source, target, sfn, tfn, ev, valid_types, can, e, r
733	) -> dict:
734	    org_name = target.replace("-", " ").lower().replace("house ", "")
735	    full_org_name = target.replace("-", " ").lower()
736	
737	    # SWORN_TO: source is feudally sworn to this org/house
738	    if can("SWORN_TO"):
739	        if _check_sworn_to_org_explicit(ev, sfn, org_name, full_org_name):
740	            return e("SWORN_TO", qualifier="current")
741	
742	    # MEMBER_OF: source belongs to this faction/order
743	    if can("MEMBER_OF"):
744	        if f"{sfn} joined" in ev and org_name in ev:
745	            return e("MEMBER_OF")
746	        if f"{sfn} is a member of" in ev and org_name in ev:
747	            return e("MEMBER_OF")
748	
749	    # OPPOSES: source opposed this org
750	    if can("OPPOSES"):
751	        if _check_opposes_explicit(ev, sfn, org_name):
752	            return e("OPPOSES")
753	
754	    # BETRAYS: source betrayed this org
755	    if can("BETRAYS"):
756	        if f"{sfn} betray" in ev and org_name in ev:
757	            return e("BETRAYS")
758	
759	    # CONSPIRES_WITH
760	    if can("CONSPIRES_WITH"):
761	        if "conspire" in ev and sfn in ev and org_name in ev:
762	            return e("CONSPIRES_WITH")
763	
764	    # ALLIES_WITH
765	    if can("ALLIES_WITH"):
766	        if _check_allies_explicit(ev, sfn, org_name):
767	            return e("ALLIES_WITH")
768	
769	    return r("organization mentioned in narrative context without typed relationship")
770	
771	
772	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
773	# Explicit relationship checkers (strict)
774	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
775	
776	def _check_sibling_explicit(ev: str, sfn: str, tfn: str) -> str | None:
777	    """
778	    Return qualifier ('full', 'half') if evidence explicitly states source and target
779	    are siblings. Returns None otherwise.
780	
781	    Strict: requires an explicit sibling phrase that names BOTH source and target
782	    in a way that unambiguously links them as siblings — not siblings of a third party.
783	
784	    Patterns accepted:
785	      A) "[source], ... [sibling_phrase], [target]" — source is described, target is their sibling
786	      B) "[target], ... [sibling_phrase], [source]" — target described, source is their sibling
787	      C) "his/her [sibling_phrase], Ser/Lord [target]" in context where source is the subject
788	
789	    NOT accepted:
790	      - "accompanied his brother, Ser [other], to X when [target] calls banners"
791	        (the sibling phrase refers to source→other, not source→target)
792	    """
793	    # Build sibling phrase → qualifier map
794	    sibling_phrases = {
795	        "older brother": "full",
796	        "younger brother": "full",
797	        "older sister": "full",
798	        "younger sister": "full",
799	        "half-brother": "half",
800	        "half brother": "half",
801	        "half-sister": "half",
802	        "half sister": "half",
803	    }
804	
805	    for phrase, qual in sibling_phrases.items():
806	        if phrase not in ev:
807	            continue
808	        if tfn not in ev:
809	            continue
810	
811	        # Find sentences containing this phrase and tfn together
812	        sentences = [s.strip() for s in ev.split(".") if s.strip()]
813	        for sent in sentences:
814	            if phrase not in sent or tfn not in sent:
815	                continue
816	            # The phrase and target are in the same sentence.
817	            # Now check: does the phrase directly precede or refer to tfn?
818	            # Pattern: "... [phrase], Ser/Lord/[tfn]" or "[phrase] [tfn]"
819	            # Accept if tfn appears immediately after the phrase (within a few words)
820	            idx_phrase = sent.find(phrase)
821	            idx_tfn = sent.find(tfn)
822	            if idx_phrase < 0 or idx_tfn < 0:
823	                continue
824	            gap_text = sent[idx_phrase + len(phrase): idx_tfn].strip()
825	            # Gap should be short (just "ser", "lord", "," etc.)
826	            gap_words = [w for w in gap_text.replace(",", "").replace("ser", "").replace("lord", "").split() if w]
827	            if len(gap_words) <= 2:
828	                # The phrase directly names the target as the sibling
829	                # Now verify that source is the one who has this sibling,
830	                # NOT a third party. We require sfn to appear in the sentence
831	                # OR for the sentence to begin the paragraph (page subject = source).
832	                if sfn in sent:
833	                    return qual
834	                # If sfn not in this sentence, check if sfn is the page subject
835	                # by verifying sfn appears as the first name in the evidence paragraph.
836	                first_sentence = sentences[0] if sentences else ""
837	                if sfn in first_sentence:
838	                    # Source is the page subject; implicit subject of this sentence
839	                    # BUT we must also check that the sentence doesn't have another
840	                    # character as the possessor of the sibling relation.
841	                    # Reject if the sentence has "[other_name]'s [phrase]" pattern
842	                    possessive_pos = sent.find("'s " + phrase)
843	                    if possessive_pos >= 0:
844	                        # Some other character owns the sibling phrase — not source
845	                        continue
846	                    # Also reject if another character name appears BEFORE the phrase
847	                    # in the sentence and source does NOT appear before the phrase
848	                    before_phrase = sent[:idx_phrase]
849	                    # Only emit if source name is present somewhere nearby
850	                    if sfn in before_phrase:
851	                        return qual
852	
853	    return None
854	
855	
856	def _check_parent_of_explicit(ev: str, sfn: str, tfn: str) -> str | None:
857	    """
858	    Source is parent of target.
859	
860	    Requires: source's son/daughter named as target, OR target's parent named as source.
861	    The target_name (tfn) must be the CHILD whose parent is source, or the specific
862	    named person whose father/mother is source.
863	
864	    NOT accepted: "source's daughter X served Y" where Y is the target (Y is not the child).
865	    """
866	    # "source's son [tfn]" or "source's daughter [tfn]" — tfn must immediately follow
867	    # the possessive phrase (the child's name should be right there, not a distant reference)
868	    for possessive in [f"{sfn}'s son", f"{sfn}'s daughter",
869	                       f"lord {sfn}'s son", f"lord {sfn}'s daughter"]:
870	        if possessive not in ev:
871	            continue
872	        # Find the possessive in the text and check tfn follows IMMEDIATELY (within ~20 chars)
873	        # This ensures "source's daughter Mara" fires for Mara but not for Alysanne
874	        # who appears later in the same sentence
875	        idx = ev.find(possessive)
876	        text_after = ev[idx + len(possessive):idx + len(possessive) + 25]
877	        # Strip guild characters (wiki markup like «»)
878	        text_after_clean = text_after.replace("«", "").replace("»", "").strip()
879	        if text_after_clean.lower().startswith(tfn) or f" {tfn}" in text_after_clean.lower():
880	            return "biological"
881	
882	    # "target's father/mother is source" patterns — target is named child
883	    for p in [f"{tfn}'s father, lord {sfn}", f"{tfn}'s father, ser {sfn}",
884	              f"{tfn}'s father is {sfn}", f"{tfn}'s mother, lady {sfn}",
885	              f"{tfn}'s mother is {sfn}"]:
886	        if p in ev:
887	            return "biological"
888	
889	    # "like his father, lord [sfn]" appearing with tfn in the same sentence
890	    # where tfn is the person saying this (tfn is the child)
891	    for p in [f"his father, lord {sfn}", f"her father, lord {sfn}",
892	              f"his father, ser {sfn}", f"her father, ser {sfn}"]:
893	        if p in ev:
894	            # Find which sentence it's in and see if tfn is subject
895	            sentences = [s.strip() for s in ev.split(".") if s.strip()]
896	            for sent in sentences:
897	                if p in sent and tfn in sent:
898	                    return "biological"
899	
900	    return None
901	
902	
903	def _check_spouse_of_explicit(ev: str, sfn: str, tfn: str) -> str | None:
904	    """
905	    Source is married to target.
906	
907	    Strict: the marriage relationship must be explicitly stated between source and target.
908	    NOT accepted: "wed off granddaughters to Freys" (Wyman arranges, not Wylis is the spouse).
909	    NOT accepted: "alliance with Stannis" (diplomatic, not marriage).
910	    """
911	    # "source's wife, [target_name]" or "source's husband, [target_name]"
912	    for p in [f"{sfn}'s wife, ", f"{sfn}'s wife.", f"{sfn}'s wife ",
913	              f"{sfn}'s husband, ", f"{sfn}'s husband.", f"{sfn}'s husband ",
914	              f"{sfn}'s late wife", f"{sfn}'s late husband"]:
915	        if p in ev and tfn in ev:
916	            # Verify they're in the same sentence
917	            sentences = [s.strip() for s in ev.split(".") if s.strip()]

984

988	        if p in ev:
989	            return True

998	                    idx_tfn = sent.find(tfn)
999	                    if idx_tfn < idx_p:
1000	                        return True
1001	
1002	    # "held captive at [location]" + target is captor mentioned in same sentence
1003	    # This is location-dependent; check carefully
1004	    if f"held captive" in ev and sfn in ev and tfn in ev:
1005	        sentences = [s.strip() for s in ev.split(".") if s.strip()]
1006	        for sent in sentences:

1011	                if (f"kept by {tfn}" in sent or f"{tfn}'s prisoner" in sent
1012	                        or f"held by {tfn}" in sent or f"captive of {tfn}" in sent):

1384	def _check_commands_in_explicit(ev: str, sfn: str, event_name: str) -> bool:
1385	    """Source commanded in the event.
1386	
1387	    Checks both single-sentence and adjacent-sentence patterns:
1388	    - Same sentence: event name + source + command signal co-occur.
1389	    - Adjacent sentences (window=2): source receives command in one sentence,
1390	      event name in the adjacent sentence — captures "Torrhen granted command to
1391	      Warrick ... The Sistermen's Rebellion ended when Warrick's fleet arrived."
1392	    """
1393	    if event_name not in ev:
1394	        return False
1395	    if sfn not in ev:
1396	        return False
1397	
1398	    command_signals = [
1399	        f"{sfn} commands", f"{sfn} commanded",
1400	        # "granted command of X army to [source]"
1401	        f"granted command of", f"command to {sfn}",
1402	    ]
1403	
1404	    sentences = [s.strip() for s in ev.split(".") if s.strip()]
1405	
1406	    # Single-sentence check
1407	    for sent in sentences:
1408	        if event_name not in sent:
1409	            continue
1410	        if sfn not in sent:
1411	            continue
1412	        if any(p in sent for p in command_signals):
1413	            return True
1414	
1415	    # Adjacent-sentence check (window of ±1 sentence)
1416	    for i, sent in enumerate(sentences):
1417	        has_command = any(p in sent for p in command_signals)
1418	        if not has_command:
1419	            continue
1420	        # Look in neighboring sentences for the event name
1421	        window = sentences[max(0, i - 1): i + 2]
1422	        for neighbor in window:
1423	            if event_name in neighbor and sfn in neighbor:
1424	                return True
1425	
1426	    return False

1448	    Source attended the event as guest/spectator.
1449	
1450	    Strict: source must be explicitly described as attending/watching the event.
1451	    NOT accepted if the event is merely mentioned in context of someone else's actions.
1452	    """
1453	    if event_name not in ev:
1454	        return False
1455	    sentences = [s.strip() for s in ev.split(".") if s.strip()]
1456	    for sent in sentences:
1457	        if sfn not in sent or event_name not in sent:
1458	            continue
1459	        # Explicit attendance language about source
1460	        if any(p in sent for p in [
1461	            f"{sfn} attended", f"{sfn} attend",
1462	            f"{sfn} spectated", f"{sfn} watched",
1463	            f"{sfn} is entertained by the",
1464	            f"{sfn} was entertained by",
1465	        ]):
1466	            return True
1467	    return False
1468	
1469	
1470	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
1471	# Main processing
1472	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
1473	
1474	def process_file(input_path: Path, output_dir: Path) -> tuple[int, int]:
1475	    """
1476	    Process one candidates file. Returns (emit_count, reject_count).
1477	
1478	    Deduplication: after classification, emitted edges are deduplicated by
1479	    (source_slug, target_slug, edge_type) — keeping the first occurrence.
1480	    Duplicates arise when the same relationship appears in multiple wiki sections
1481	    (e.g., "Narrative Arc / AGOT" and "Quotes by Wylis" both describe the
1482	    same SIBLING_OF relationship).
1483	    """
1484	    slug = input_path.stem.replace(".candidates", "")
1485	    output_path = output_dir / f"{slug}.edges.jsonl"
1486	
1487	    all_decisions: list[dict] = []
1488	    seen_emit_keys: set[tuple] = set()
1489	
1490	    with input_path.open("r", encoding="utf-8") as fin:
1491	        for line_num, line in enumerate(fin, 1):
1492	            line = line.strip()
1493	            if not line:
1494	                continue
1495	
1496	            try:
1497	                row = json.loads(line)
1498	            except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
1499	                print(
1500	                    f"  WARNING: line {line_num} in {input_path.name} invalid JSON: {exc}",
1501	                    file=sys.stderr,
1502	                )
1503	                continue
1504	
1505	            try:
1506	                decision = classify(row)
1507	            except Exception as exc:
1508	                print(
1509	                    f"  WARNING: classification error line {line_num} in {input_path.name}: {exc}",
1510	                    file=sys.stderr,
1511	                )
1512	                src = row.get("source_slug", "unknown")
1513	                tgt = row.get("target_slug", "unknown")
1514	                decision = reject(src, tgt, f"classification-error: {exc}")
1515	
1516	            # Deduplicate emit_edge rows by (source, target, edge_type)
1517	            if decision["decision"] == "emit_edge":
1518	                dedup_key = (
1519	                    decision["source_slug"],
1520	                    decision["target_slug"],
1521	                    decision["edge_type"],
1522	                )
1523	                if dedup_key in seen_emit_keys:
1524	                    # Convert to reject with dedup reason instead of writing duplicate
1525	                    decision = reject(
1526	                        decision["source_slug"],
1527	                        decision["target_slug"],
1528	                        "duplicate-edge-across-sections",
1529	                    )
1530	                else:
1531	                    seen_emit_keys.add(dedup_key)
1532	
1533	            all_decisions.append(decision)
1534	
1535	    emit_count = sum(1 for d in all_decisions if d["decision"] == "emit_edge")
1536	    reject_count = sum(1 for d in all_decisions if d["decision"] != "emit_edge")
1537	
1538	    with output_path.open("w", encoding="utf-8") as fout:
1539	        for decision in all_decisions:
1540	            fout.write(json.dumps(decision, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
1541	
1542	    return emit_count, reject_count
1543	
1544	
1545	def main() -> None:
1546	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
1547	        description="Classify enriched Manderly prose-edge candidates into emit/reject decisions."
1548	    )
1549	    parser.add_argument(
1550	        "--input-dir",
1551	        type=Path,
1552	        default=INPUT_DIR,
1553	        help="Directory containing *.candidates.jsonl files",
1554	    )
1555	    parser.add_argument(
1556	        "--output-dir",
1557	        type=Path,
1558	        default=OUTPUT_DIR,
1559	        help="Directory to write *.edges.jsonl output files",
1560	    )
1561	    parser.add_argument(
1562	        "--files",
1563	        nargs="+",
1564	        default=TARGET_FILES,
1565	        help="Specific candidate filenames to process (default: all 5 Manderly files)",
1566	    )
1567	    args = parser.parse_args()
1568	
1569	    args.output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
1570	
1571	    total_emit = 0
1572	    total_reject = 0
1573	
1574	    for filename in args.files:
1575	        input_path = args.input_dir / filename
1576	        if not input_path.exists():
1577	            print(f"  WARNING: {input_path} not found — skipping", file=sys.stderr)
1578	            continue
1579	
1580	        emit_count, reject_count = process_file(input_path, args.output_dir)
1581	        total_emit += emit_count
1582	        total_reject += reject_count
1583	        print(
1584	            f"[done] {filename} → {emit_count} emit_edge, {reject_count} reject_just_mention"
1585	        )
1586	
1587	    print()
1588	    print(f"Summary: {len(args.files)} files processed")
1589	    print(f"  Total emit_edge:           {total_emit}")
1590	    print(f"  Total reject_just_mention: {total_reject}")
1591	    print(f"  Total rows:                {total_emit + total_reject}")
